00027 /*=========================================================================
00028  * SYSTEM:    Verifier
00029  * SUBSYSTEM: Verifies codes within a method block.
00030  * FILE:      check_code.c
00031  * OVERVIEW:  Verifies that the code within a method block does not
00032  *            exploit any security holes.
00033  *            Initial implementation based on the Classic VM Verifier.
00034  *=======================================================================*/
00035

00135 /*
00136  * Access local hash tables without locking. This extra level
00137  * of naming is intended to emphasize the fact that locks are
00138  * not held and also to do error handling. Although it is not
00139  * strictly necessary, one should always use the "_Local"
00140  * versions of these functions on the verifier's local hash
00141  * tables.
00142  */
00143 #define Str2IDFree_Local(localHashRoot) Str2IDFree(localHashRoot)
00144 
00145 /*=========================================================================
00146  * FUNCTION:      Str2ID_Local
00147  * OVERVIEW:      Access local hash tables without locking
00148  *                This extra level of naming is intended to emphasize the
00149  *                fact that locks are not held and also to do error handling.
00150  *                These functions could all be macros like Str2IDFree_Local(),
00151  *                except that Str2ID() and ID2Str() can run out of memory,
00152  *                and the code in this file makes it inconvenient to check
00153  *                for that. As an expedient, rather than throwing exceptions,
00154  *                Str2ID_Local() is a function that calls CCerror() if
00155  *                necessary.
00156  *                Returns the index given the string which corresponds to the
00157  *                hash table entry.
00158  * INTERFACE:
00159  *   parameters:  context_type *: context
00160  *                struct StrIDhash **: hash_ptr
00161  *                char *: s
00162  *                void ***: param
00163  *                int: Copy
00164  *
00165  *   returns:     unsigned short
00166  *=======================================================================*/
00167 unsigned short
00168 Str2ID_Local(context_type *context, struct StrIDhash **hash_ptr, char *s,
00169          void ***param, int Copy) {
00170     unsigned short ret;
00171     if ((ret = Str2ID(hash_ptr, s, param, Copy)) == 0) {
00172     CCerror(context, "Out of memory");
00173     }
00174 
00175     return ret;
00176 }
00177 
00178 /*=========================================================================
00179  * FUNCTION:      ID2Str_Local
00180  * OVERVIEW:      Access local hash tables without locking
00181  *                This extra level of naming is intended to emphasize the
00182  *                fact that locks are not held and also to do error handling.
00183  *                These functions could all be macros like Str2IDFree_Local(),
00184  *                except that Str2ID() and ID2Str() can run out of memory,
00185  *                and the code in this file makes it inconvenient to check
00186  *                for that. As an expedient, rather than throwing exceptions,
00187  *                ID2Str_Local() is a function that calls CCerror() if
00188  *                necessary.
00189  *                Returns the string given the index which corresponds to the
00190  *                hash table entry.
00191  * INTERFACE:
00192  *   parameters:  context_type *: context
00193  *                struct StrIDhash *: h
00194  *                unsigned short: ID
00195  *                void ***: param
00196  *
00197  *   returns:     char *
00198  *=======================================================================*/
00199 char *
00200 ID2Str_Local(context_type *context, struct StrIDhash *h, unsigned short ID,
00201          void ***param) {
00202     char *ret;
00203     if ((ret = ID2Str(h, ID, param)) == 0) {
00204     CCerror(context, "Out of memory");
00205     }
00206 
00207     return ret;
00208 }
00209 
00210 /*=========================================================================
00211  * FUNCTION:      verify_class_codes
00212  * OVERVIEW:      Verifies the code for each of the methods in a class.
00213  *                Invoked by verify_class().
00214  *                Returns true if the class codes are ok.
00215  * INTERFACE:
00216  *   parameters:  pointer to the ClassClass structure.
00217  *
00218  *   returns:     boolean type
00219  *=======================================================================*/
00220 bool verify_class_codes(ClassClass *cb) {
00221     context_type context_structure;
00222     context_type *context = &context_structure;
00223     bool result = true;
00224     void **addr;
00225     int i;
00226 
00227 #ifdef DEBUG_VERIFIER
00228     GlobalContext = context;
00229 #endif
00230 
00231     /* Initialize the class-wide fields of the context. */
00232     context->class = cb;
00233 
00234     context->classHash = 0;
00235     /* Zero method block field of the context, in case anyone calls CCerrror */
00236     context->mb = 0;
00237     context->superClasses = NULL;    /* filled in later */
00238 
00239     /* Don't call CCerror or anything that can call it above the setjmp! */
00240     if (!setjmp(context->jump_buffer)) {
00241     struct methodblock *mb;
00242     struct fieldblock *fb;
00243 
00244     CCinit(context);        /* initialize heap; may throw */
00245 
00246     context->object_info =
00247         MAKE_CLASSNAME_INFO(context, JAVAPKG "Object", &addr);
00248     *addr = classJavaLangObject;
00249     context->string_info =
00250         MAKE_CLASSNAME_INFO(context, JAVAPKG "String", &addr);
00251     *addr = classJavaLangString;
00252     context->throwable_info =
00253         MAKE_CLASSNAME_INFO(context, JAVAPKG "Throwable", &addr);
00254     *addr = classJavaLangThrowable;
00255     context->currentclass_info =
00256         MAKE_CLASSNAME_INFO_WITH_COPY(context, cbName(cb), &addr);
00257     *addr = cb;
00258     if (cbSuperclass(cb) != 0) {
00259         ClassClass *super = cbSuperclass(cb);
00260         context->superclass_info =
00261             MAKE_CLASSNAME_INFO_WITH_COPY(context, cbName(super), &addr);
00262         *addr = super;
00263     } else {
00264         context->superclass_info = 0;
00265     }
00266 
00267     /* Look at each method */
00268     for (i = cbFieldsCount(cb), fb = cbFields(cb); --i >= 0; fb++)
00269         verify_field(context, fb);
00270 
00271         unhand(context->class)->new_class_entries =
00272         (char **)malloc((cbConstantPoolCount(context->class) * 3 + 100) * sizeof(char *));
00273         unhand(context->class)->n_new_class_entries = 0;
00274 
00275     for (i = cbMethodsCount(cb), mb = cbMethods(cb); --i >= 0; mb++)
00276         verify_method(context, mb);
00277 
00278     unhand(context->class)->new_class_entries =
00279         (char **)realloc(unhand(context->class)->new_class_entries,
00280                          unhand(context->class)->n_new_class_entries * sizeof(char *));
00281 
00282     result = true;
00283     } else {
00284     result = false;
00285     }
00286 
00287     /* Cleanup */
00288     Str2IDFree_Local(&context->classHash);
00289 
00290 #ifdef DEBUG_VERIFIER
00291     GlobalContext = 0;
00292 #endif
00293 
00294     if (context->superClasses != NULL) {
00295     sysFree(context->superClasses);
00296     }
00297     CCdestroy(context);        /* destroy heap */
00298     return result;
00299 }
00300 
00301 /*=========================================================================
00302  * FUNCTION:      verify_field
00303  * OVERVIEW:      Verifies the field block within each method of a class
00304  *                file.
00305  *                Invoked by verify_class_codes().
00306  *                Returns true if the field block is ok.
00307  * INTERFACE:
00308  *   parameters:  pointer to the context_type structure.
00309  *                pointer to the field block
00310  *
00311  *   returns:     boolean type
00312  *=======================================================================*/
00313 static void
00314 verify_field(context_type *context, struct fieldblock *fb)
00315 {
00316     int access_bits = fb->access;
00317 
00318     if (  ((access_bits & ACC_PUBLIC) != 0) &&
00319       ((access_bits & (ACC_PRIVATE | ACC_PROTECTED)) != 0)) {
00320     if (verbose) {
00321         jio_fprintf(stderr, "VERIFIER ERROR %s.%s:\n",
00322             cbName(fieldclass(fb)), fb->name);
00323         jio_fprintf(stderr, "Inconsistent access bits.");
00324     }
00325     longjmp(context->jump_buffer, 1);
00326     }
00327 }
00328 
00329 /*=========================================================================
00330  * FUNCTION:      get_type_name
00331  * OVERVIEW:      Retrieves the type name information given the item type.
00332  *                Used by get_type_code() which is invoked by verify_method()
00333  *                to retrieve the type code for the stack map entries.
00334  * INTERFACE:
00335  *   parameters:  pointer to the context_type structure.
00336  *                fullinfo_type: type
00337  *                char *: buf
00338  *
00339  *   returns:     nothing
00340  *=======================================================================*/
00341 static void
00342 get_type_name(context_type *context, fullinfo_type type, char *buf)
00343 {
00344     int i;
00345     int indirection = GET_INDIRECTION(type);
00346     for (i = indirection; i-- > 0; )
00347     *buf++ = '[';
00348     switch (GET_ITEM_TYPE(type)) {
00349         case ITEM_Integer:
00350         *buf++ = 'I'; break;
00351     case ITEM_Float:
00352         *buf++ = 'F'; break;
00353     case ITEM_Double:
00354         *buf++ = 'D'; break;
00355     case ITEM_Long:
00356         *buf++ = 'J'; break;
00357     case ITEM_Char:
00358         *buf++ = 'C'; break;
00359     case ITEM_Short:
00360         *buf++ = 'S'; break;
00361     case ITEM_Byte:
00362         *buf++ = 'B'; break;
00363     case ITEM_Boolean:
00364         *buf++ = 'Z'; break;
00365     case ITEM_Object: {
00366             unsigned short extra = GET_EXTRA_INFO(type);
00367             if (indirection) *buf++ = 'L';
00368             if (extra == 0) {
00369                 panic("unexpected");
00370             } else {
00371                 char *name = ID2Str_Local(context, context->classHash,
00372                                           extra, 0);
00373                 strcpy(buf, name);
00374                 if (indirection) strcat(buf, ";");
00375             }
00376             return;
00377         }
00378         default:
00379             panic("bad type");
00380     }
00381     *buf = '\0';
00382 }
00383 
00384 /*=========================================================================
00385  * FUNCTION:      check_class_constant
00386  * OVERVIEW:      Checks and returns the index corresponding to the given
00387  *                UTF8 constant entry within the constant pool.
00388  *                Returns -1 if none was found.
00389  *                Invoked by get_type_code().
00390  * INTERFACE:
00391  *   parameters:  pointer to the ClassClass structure.
00392  *                char *: utf8
00393  *
00394  *   returns:     long type
00395  *=======================================================================*/
00396 long check_class_constant(ClassClass *cb, char *utf8)
00397 {
00398     int i;
00399     cp_item_type *constant_pool = cbConstantPool(cb);
00400     unsigned char *type_table =
00401         constant_pool[CONSTANT_POOL_TYPE_TABLE_INDEX].type;
00402     for (i = 0; i < cbConstantPoolCount(cb); i++) {
00403     if (type_table[i] == (CONSTANT_Class | CONSTANT_POOL_ENTRY_RESOLVED) &&
00404         strcmp(utf8, cbName(constant_pool[i].clazz)) == 0) {
00405         return i;
00406     }
00407     if (type_table[i] == CONSTANT_Class &&
00408         strcmp(utf8, constant_pool[constant_pool[i].i].cp) == 0) {
00409         return i;
00410     }
00411     }
00412     return -1;
00413 }
00414 
00415 /*=========================================================================
00416  * FUNCTION:      get_type_code
00417  * OVERVIEW:      Retrieves the type code entry given the item type.
00418  *                Invoked by verify_method() to retrieve the type code
00419  *                for the stack map entries.
00420  * INTERFACE:
00421  *   parameters:  pointer to the context_type structure.
00422  *                fullinfo_type: type
00423  *
00424  *   returns:     struct map_entry
00425  *=======================================================================*/
00426 struct map_entry get_type_code(context_type *context, fullinfo_type type)
00427 {
00428     struct map_entry result = {0,0};
00429     switch (type) {
00430         case ITEM_Double_2:
00431         case ITEM_Long_2:
00432         case ITEM_Bogus:
00433         result.type = CF_ITEM_Bogus;
00434         return result;
00435     case ITEM_Integer:
00436         result.type = CF_ITEM_Integer;
00437         return result;
00438     case ITEM_Float:
00439         result.type = CF_ITEM_Float;
00440         return result;
00441     case ITEM_Double:
00442         result.type = CF_ITEM_Double;
00443         return result;
00444     case ITEM_Long:
00445         result.type = CF_ITEM_Long;
00446         return result;
00447     case ITEM_InitObject:
00448         result.type = CF_ITEM_InitObject;
00449         return result;
00450     default:
00451     if (GET_ITEM_TYPE(type) == ITEM_NewObject) {
00452         int inum = GET_EXTRA_INFO(type);
00453         result.type = CF_ITEM_NewObject;
00454         result.info = context->instruction_data[inum].offset;
00455         return result;
00456     } else if (GET_ITEM_TYPE(type) == ITEM_Object && GET_EXTRA_INFO(type) == 0) {
00457         result.type = CF_ITEM_Null;
00458         return result;
00459         } else if (GET_ITEM_TYPE(type) == ITEM_Object || GET_INDIRECTION(type) > 0) {
00460         char type_name[1024];
00461         int i;
00462             result.type = CF_ITEM_Object;
00463         get_type_name(context, type, type_name);
00464         i = check_class_constant(context->class, type_name);
00465         if (i >= 0) {
00466         result.info = i;
00467         return result;
00468         }
00469         for (i = 0; i < unhand(context->class)->n_new_class_entries; i++) {
00470         if (strcmp(type_name, unhand(context->class)->new_class_entries[i]) == 0) {
00471             result.info = ~i;
00472             return result;
00473         }
00474         }
00475         {
00476         int entries = unhand(context->class)->n_new_class_entries;
00477         unhand(context->class)->new_class_entries[entries] = strdup(type_name);
00478         result.info = ~entries;
00479         unhand(context->class)->n_new_class_entries = entries + 1;
00480         return result;
00481         }
00482     } else {
00483         panic("bad type code");
00484         return result; /* not reached */
00485     }
00486     }
00487 }
00488 
00489 /*=========================================================================
00490  * FUNCTION:      verify_method
00491  * OVERVIEW:      Verifies the code for one method within a class file.
00492  *                Invoked by verify_class_codes().
00493  * INTERFACE:
00494  *   parameters:  pointer to the context_type structure.
00495  *                struct methodblock*: mb
00496  *
00497  *   returns:     nothing
00498  *=======================================================================*/
00499 static void
00500 verify_method(context_type *context, struct methodblock *mb)
00501 {
00502     int access_bits = mb->fb.access;
00503     unsigned char *code;
00504     int code_length;
00505     short *code_data;
00506     instruction_data_type *idata = 0;
00507     int instruction_count;
00508     int i, offset, inumber;
00509     int exception_count;
00510     int n_stack_maps, dead_count, jsr_count;
00511 
00512  again:
00513     code = mb->code;
00514     code_length = mb->code_length;
00515 
00516     /* CCerror can give method-specific info once this is set */
00517     context->mb = mb;
00518 
00519     CCreinit(context);        /* initial heap */
00520     code_data = NEW(short, code_length);
00521 
00522 #ifdef DEBUG_VERIFIER
00523     if (verify_verbose) {
00524     jio_fprintf(stdout, "Looking at %s.%s%s 0x%x\n",
00525            cbName(fieldclass(&mb->fb)), mb->fb.name, mb->fb.signature,
00526            (long)mb);
00527     }
00528 #endif
00529 
00530     if (((access_bits & ACC_PUBLIC) != 0) &&
00531     ((access_bits & (ACC_PRIVATE | ACC_PROTECTED)) != 0)) {
00532     CCerror(context, "Inconsistent access bits.");
00533     }
00534 
00535     if ((access_bits & (ACC_ABSTRACT | ACC_NATIVE)) != 0) {
00536     /* not much to do for abstract or native methods */
00537         return;
00538     }
00539 
00540     if (code_length >= 65535) {
00541     CCerror(context, "Code of a method longer than 65535 bytes");
00542     }
00543 
00544     /* Run through the code.  Mark the start of each instruction, and give
00545      * the instruction a number */
00546     for (i = 0, offset = 0; offset < code_length; i++) {
00547     int length = instruction_length(&code[offset]);
00548     int next_offset = offset + length;
00549     if (length <= 0)
00550         CCerror(context, "Illegal instruction found at offset %d", offset);
00551     if (next_offset > code_length)
00552         CCerror(context, "Code stops in the middle of instruction "
00553             " starting at offset %d", offset);
00554     code_data[offset] = i;
00555     while (++offset < next_offset)
00556         code_data[offset] = -1; /* illegal location */
00557     }
00558     instruction_count = i;    /* number of instructions in code */
00559 
00560     /* Allocate a structure to hold info about each instruction. */
00561     idata = NEW(instruction_data_type, instruction_count);
00562 
00563     /* Initialize the heap, and other info in the context structure. */
00564     context->code = code;
00565     context->instruction_data = idata;
00566     context->code_data = code_data;
00567     context->instruction_count = instruction_count;
00568     context->handler_info = NEW(struct handler_info_type,
00569                 mb->exception_table_length);
00570     context->bitmask_size = (mb->nlocals + (BITS_PER_INT - 1))/BITS_PER_INT;
00571 
00572     if (instruction_count == 0)
00573     CCerror(context, "Empty code");
00574 
00575     for (inumber = 0, offset = 0; offset < code_length; inumber++) {
00576     int length = instruction_length(&code[offset]);
00577     instruction_data_type *this_idata = &idata[inumber];
00578     this_idata->opcode = code[offset];
00579     this_idata->offset = offset;
00580     this_idata->length = length;
00581     this_idata->stack_info.stack = NULL;
00582     this_idata->stack_info.stack_size  = UNKNOWN_STACK_SIZE;
00583     this_idata->register_info.register_count = UNKNOWN_REGISTER_COUNT;
00584     this_idata->changed = false;  /* no need to look at it yet. */
00585     this_idata->protected = false;  /* no need to look at it yet. */
00586         /* Added for inlinejsr */
00587         this_idata->is_target = false;  /* no need to look at it yet. */
00588     this_idata->and_flags = (flag_type) -1;    /* "bottom" and value */
00589     this_idata->or_flags = 0; /* "bottom" or value*/
00590 
00591     /* This also sets up this_data->operand.  It also makes the
00592      * xload_x and xstore_x instructions look like the generic form. */
00593     verify_opcode_operands(context, inumber, offset);
00594     offset += length;
00595     }
00596 
00597 
00598     /* make sure exception table is reasonable. */
00599     initialize_exception_table(context);
00600     /* Set up first instruction, and start of exception handlers. */
00601     initialize_dataflow(context);
00602     /* Run data flow analysis on the instructions. */
00603     context->redoJsr = false;
00604     run_dataflow(context);
00605 
00606     for (inumber = 0; inumber < instruction_count; inumber++) {
00607         instruction_data_type *this_idata = &idata[inumber];
00608         if (  (this_idata->or_flags & FLAG_REACHED) &&
00609               ((this_idata->opcode == opc_jsr) ||
00610                (this_idata->opcode == opc_jsr_w)) &&
00611               (this_idata->operand2.i == UNKNOWN_RET_INSTRUCTION)) {
00612             this_idata->changed = true;
00613             context->redoJsr = true;
00614         }
00615     }
00616     if (context->redoJsr) {
00617         run_dataflow(context);
00618     }
00619 
00620     /* verify checked exceptions, if any */
00621     if ((exception_count = mb->nexceptions) > 0) {
00622     unsigned short *exceptions = mb->exceptions;
00623     for (i = 0; i < (int)exception_count; i++) {
00624         /* Make sure the constant pool item is CONSTANT_Class */
00625         verify_constant_pool_type(context, (int)exceptions[i],
00626         1 << CONSTANT_Class);
00627     }
00628     }
00629 
00630 
00631     dead_count = jsr_count = 0;
00632     for (inumber = 0; inumber < instruction_count; inumber++) {
00633         instruction_data_type *this_idata = &idata[inumber];
00634         if ((this_idata->or_flags & FLAG_REACHED) == 0) {
00635             dead_count++;
00636         } else if (this_idata->opcode == opc_jsr ||
00637                    this_idata->opcode == opc_jsr_w) {
00638             jsr_count++;
00639         }
00640     }
00641     if (dead_count > 0 || jsr_count > 0) {
00642         rewriteCode(context, mb);
00643         goto again;
00644     }
00645 
00646     n_stack_maps = 0;
00647     for (inumber = 0; inumber < instruction_count; inumber++) {
00648         instruction_data_type *this_idata = &idata[inumber];
00649         if (this_idata->is_target) {
00650             n_stack_maps++;
00651         }
00652     }
00653 
00654     mb->n_stack_maps = n_stack_maps;
00655     mb->stack_maps =
00656         (struct stack_map *)malloc(n_stack_maps * sizeof(struct stack_map));
00657 
00658     n_stack_maps = 0;
00659     for (inumber = 0; inumber < instruction_count; inumber++) {
00660     instruction_data_type *this_idata = &idata[inumber];
00661     if (this_idata->is_target) {
00662             struct stack_info_type *stack_info = &this_idata->stack_info;
00663             struct register_info_type *register_info = &this_idata->register_info;
00664             int register_count = register_info->register_count;
00665           struct stack_item_type *stack;
00666         struct map_entry *new_entries;
00667         int index, index2;
00668 
00669             /* We may be allocating too big a structure if there are longs
00670              * or doubles on the stack, but that's okay */
00671         new_entries = (struct map_entry *)malloc(register_count * sizeof(struct map_entry));
00672 
00673         for (index2 = 0, index = 0; index < register_count; index++) {
00674                 fullinfo_type info = register_info->registers[index];
00675                 if (info == ITEM_Double || info == ITEM_Long) {
00676                     if (index + 1 < register_count &&
00677                            register_info->registers[index + 1] == info+1) {
00678                         new_entries[index2++] = get_type_code(context, info);
00679                         index++;
00680                     } else {
00681                         new_entries[index2++] = get_type_code(context, ITEM_Bogus);
00682                     }
00683                 } else {
00684                     new_entries[index2++] = get_type_code(context, info);
00685                 }
00686             }
00687             mb->stack_maps[n_stack_maps].offset = this_idata->offset;
00688         mb->stack_maps[n_stack_maps].locals = new_entries;
00689         mb->stack_maps[n_stack_maps].nlocals = index2;
00690 
00691         mb->stack_maps[n_stack_maps].nstacks = 0;
00692         for (stack = stack_info->stack; stack; stack = stack->next) {
00693         mb->stack_maps[n_stack_maps].nstacks++;
00694         }
00695         new_entries = (struct map_entry *)malloc(mb->stack_maps[n_stack_maps].nstacks * sizeof(struct map_entry));
00696         index = 0;
00697             for (stack = stack_info->stack; stack; stack = stack->next) {
00698         new_entries[mb->stack_maps[n_stack_maps].nstacks - (++index)] =
00699             get_type_code(context, stack->item);
00700         }
00701             mb->stack_maps[n_stack_maps].stacks = new_entries;
00702 
00703         unhand(context->class)->has_stack_maps = 1;
00704         n_stack_maps++;
00705     }
00706     }
00707 }
00708 
00709 /*=========================================================================
00710  * FUNCTION:      verify_opcode_operands
00711  * OVERVIEW:      Verifies the operands of a single instruction given an
00712  *                instruction number and an offset.
00713  *                Also, for simplicity, move the operand into the ->operand
00714  *                field.
00715  *                Make sure that branches do not go into the middle
00716  *                of nowhere.
00717  *                Invoked by verify_method().
00718  * INTERFACE:
00719  *   parameters:  context_type *: context
00720  *                int: inumber
00721  *                int: offset
00722  *
00723  *   returns:     nothing
00724  *=======================================================================*/
00725 static void
00726 verify_opcode_operands(context_type *context, int inumber, int offset)
00727 {
00728     instruction_data_type *idata = context->instruction_data;
00729     instruction_data_type *this_idata = &idata[inumber];
00730     short *code_data = context->code_data;
00731     struct methodblock *mb = context->mb;
00732     unsigned char *code = context->code;
00733     opcode_type opcode = this_idata->opcode;
00734     int var;
00735 
00736     this_idata->operand.i = 0;
00737     this_idata->operand2.i = 0;
00738 
00739     switch (opcode) {
00740 
00741     case opc_jsr:
00742     /* instruction of ret statement */
00743     this_idata->operand2.i = UNKNOWN_RET_INSTRUCTION;
00744     /* FALLTHROUGH */
00745     case opc_ifeq: case opc_ifne: case opc_iflt:
00746     case opc_ifge: case opc_ifgt: case opc_ifle:
00747     case opc_ifnull: case opc_ifnonnull:
00748     case opc_if_icmpeq: case opc_if_icmpne: case opc_if_icmplt:
00749     case opc_if_icmpge: case opc_if_icmpgt: case opc_if_icmple:
00750     case opc_if_acmpeq: case opc_if_acmpne:
00751     case opc_goto: {
00752     /* Set the ->operand to be the instruction number of the target. */
00753     int jump = (((signed char)(code[offset+1])) << 8) + code[offset+2];
00754     int target = offset + jump;
00755     if (!isLegalTarget(context, target))
00756         CCerror(context, "Illegal target of jump or branch");
00757     this_idata->operand.i = code_data[target];
00758     break;
00759     }
00760 
00761     case opc_jsr_w:
00762     /* instruction of ret statement */
00763     this_idata->operand2.i = UNKNOWN_RET_INSTRUCTION;
00764     /* FALLTHROUGH */
00765     case opc_goto_w: {
00766     /* Set the ->operand to be the instruction number of the target. */
00767     int jump = (((signed char)(code[offset+1])) << 24) +
00768                  (code[offset+2] << 16) + (code[offset+3] << 8) +
00769                  (code[offset + 4]);
00770     int target = offset + jump;
00771     if (!isLegalTarget(context, target))
00772         CCerror(context, "Illegal target of jump or branch");
00773     this_idata->operand.i = code_data[target];
00774     break;
00775     }
00776 
00777     case opc_tableswitch:
00778     case opc_lookupswitch: {
00779     /* Set the ->operand to be a table of possible instruction targets. */
00780     uint32_t *lpc = (uint32_t *) UCALIGN(code + offset + 1);
00781     uint32_t *lptr;
00782     int *saved_operand;
00783     int keys;
00784     int k, delta;
00785     if (opcode == opc_tableswitch) {
00786         keys = ntohl(lpc[2]) -  ntohl(lpc[1]) + 1;
00787         delta = 1;
00788     } else {
00789         keys = ntohl(lpc[1]); /* number of pairs */
00790         delta = 2;
00791         /* Make sure that the tableswitch items are sorted */
00792         for (k = keys - 1, lptr = &lpc[2]; --k >= 0; lptr += 2) {
00793         uint32_t this_key = ntohl(lptr[0]);    /* NB: ntohl may be unsigned */
00794         uint32_t next_key = ntohl(lptr[2]);
00795         if (this_key >= next_key) {
00796             CCerror(context, "Unsorted lookup switch");
00797         }
00798         }
00799     }
00800         /* This code has been changed for inlining.   We know have the keys
00801          * in the same order that they occur in the code, with the default
00802          * key being the first one.
00803          */
00804     saved_operand = NEW(int, keys + 2);
00805     if (!isLegalTarget(context, offset + ntohl(lpc[0])))
00806         CCerror(context, "Illegal default target in switch");
00807 
00808         saved_operand[0] = keys + 1; /* number of successors */
00809     saved_operand[1] = code_data[offset + ntohl(lpc[0])]; /* default */
00810 
00811     for (k = 0, lptr = &lpc[3]; k < keys; lptr += delta, k++) {
00812         int target = offset + ntohl(lptr[0]);
00813         if (!isLegalTarget(context, target))
00814         CCerror(context, "Illegal branch in opc_tableswitch");
00815         saved_operand[k + 2] = code_data[target];
00816     }
00817     this_idata->operand.ip = saved_operand;
00818     break;
00819     }
00820 
00821     case opc_ldc: {
00822     /* Make sure the constant pool item is the right type. */
00823     int key = code[offset + 1];
00824     int types = (1 << CONSTANT_Integer) | (1 << CONSTANT_Float) |
00825                         (1 << CONSTANT_String);
00826     this_idata->operand.i = key;
00827     verify_constant_pool_type(context, key, types);
00828     break;
00829     }
00830 
00831     case opc_ldc_w: {
00832     /* Make sure the constant pool item is the right type. */
00833     int key = (code[offset + 1] << 8) + code[offset + 2];
00834     int types = (1 << CONSTANT_Integer) | (1 << CONSTANT_Float) |
00835         (1 << CONSTANT_String);
00836     this_idata->operand.i = key;
00837     verify_constant_pool_type(context, key, types);
00838     break;
00839     }
00840 
00841     case opc_ldc2_w: {
00842     /* Make sure the constant pool item is the right type. */
00843     int key = (code[offset + 1] << 8) + code[offset + 2];
00844     int types = (1 << CONSTANT_Double) | (1 << CONSTANT_Long);
00845     this_idata->operand.i = key;
00846     verify_constant_pool_type(context, key, types);
00847     break;
00848     }
00849 
00850     case opc_getfield: case opc_putfield:
00851     case opc_getstatic: case opc_putstatic: {
00852     /* Make sure the constant pool item is the right type. */
00853     int key = (code[offset + 1] << 8) + code[offset + 2];
00854     this_idata->operand.i = key;
00855     verify_constant_pool_type(context, key, 1 << CONSTANT_Fieldref);
00856     if (opcode == opc_getfield || opcode == opc_putfield)
00857         set_protected(context, inumber, key, opcode);
00858     break;
00859     }
00860 
00861     case opc_invokevirtual:
00862     case opc_invokespecial:
00863     case opc_invokestatic:
00864     case opc_invokeinterface: {
00865     /* Make sure the constant pool item is the right type. */
00866     int key = (code[offset + 1] << 8) + code[offset + 2];
00867     char *methodname;
00868     fullinfo_type clazz_info;
00869     int kind = (opcode == opc_invokeinterface
00870                         ? 1 << CONSTANT_InterfaceMethodref
00871                         : 1 << CONSTANT_Methodref);
00872     /* Make sure the constant pool item is the right type. */
00873     verify_constant_pool_type(context, key, kind);
00874     methodname = cp_index_to_fieldname(context, key);
00875     clazz_info = cp_index_to_class_fullinfo(context, key, true);
00876     this_idata->operand.i = key;
00877     this_idata->operand2.fi = clazz_info;
00878     if (strcmp(methodname, "<init>") == 0) {
00879         if (opcode != opc_invokespecial)
00880         CCerror(context,
00881             "Must call initializers using invokespecial");
00882         this_idata->opcode = opc_invokeinit;
00883     } else {
00884         if (methodname[0] == '<')
00885         CCerror(context, "Illegal call to internal method");
00886         if (opcode == opc_invokespecial
00887            && clazz_info != context->currentclass_info
00888            && clazz_info != context->superclass_info) {
00889         ClassClass *cb = context->class;
00890         for (; ; cb = cbSuperclass(cb)) {
00891             if (clazz_info == MAKE_CLASSNAME_INFO_WITH_COPY(context, cbName(cb), 0))
00892             break;
00893             /* The optimizer make cause this to happen on local code */
00894             if (cbSuperclass(cb) == 0) {
00895             /* optimizer make cause this to happen on local code */
00896             if (cbLoader(cb) != 0)
00897                 CCerror(context,
00898                     "Illegal use of nonvirtual function call");
00899             break;
00900             }
00901         }
00902         }
00903     }
00904     if (opcode == opc_invokeinterface) {
00905         char *signature = cp_index_to_signature(context, key);
00906         unsigned int args1 = Signature2ArgsSize(signature) + 1;
00907         unsigned int args2 = code[offset + 3];
00908         if (args1 != args2) {
00909         CCerror(context,
00910             "Inconsistent args_size for opc_invokeinterface");
00911         }
00912             if (code[offset + 4] != 0) {
00913                 CCerror(context,
00914                         "Fourth operand byte of invokeinterface must be zero");
00915             }
00916     } else if (opcode == opc_invokevirtual
00917               || opcode == opc_invokespecial)
00918         set_protected(context, inumber, key, opcode);
00919     break;
00920     }
00921 
00922 
00923     case opc_instanceof:
00924     case opc_checkcast:
00925     case opc_new:
00926     case opc_anewarray:
00927     case opc_multianewarray: {
00928     /* Make sure the constant pool item is a class */
00929     int key = (code[offset + 1] << 8) + code[offset + 2];
00930     fullinfo_type target;
00931     verify_constant_pool_type(context, key, 1 << CONSTANT_Class);
00932     target = cp_index_to_class_fullinfo(context, key, false);
00933     if (GET_ITEM_TYPE(target) == ITEM_Bogus)
00934         CCerror(context, "Illegal type");
00935     switch(opcode) {
00936     case opc_anewarray:
00937         if ((GET_INDIRECTION(target)) >= MAX_ARRAY_DIMENSIONS)
00938         CCerror(context, "Array with too many dimensions");
00939         this_idata->operand.fi = MAKE_FULLINFO(GET_ITEM_TYPE(target),
00940                            GET_INDIRECTION(target) + 1,
00941                            GET_EXTRA_INFO(target));
00942         break;
00943     case opc_new:
00944         if (WITH_ZERO_EXTRA_INFO(target) !=
00945                      MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_Object, 0, 0))
00946         CCerror(context, "Illegal creation of multi-dimensional array");
00947         /* operand gets set to the "unitialized object".  operand2 gets
00948          * set to what the value will be after it's initialized. */
00949         this_idata->operand.fi = MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_NewObject, 0, inumber);
00950         this_idata->operand2.fi = target;
00951         break;
00952     case opc_multianewarray:
00953         this_idata->operand.fi = target;
00954         this_idata->operand2.i = code[offset + 3];
00955         if (    (this_idata->operand2.i > (int)GET_INDIRECTION(target))
00956          || (this_idata->operand2.i == 0))
00957         CCerror(context, "Illegal dimension argument");
00958         break;
00959     default:
00960         this_idata->operand.fi = target;
00961     }
00962     break;
00963     }
00964 
00965     case opc_newarray: {
00966     /* Cache the result of the opc_newarray into the operand slot */
00967     fullinfo_type full_info;
00968     switch (code[offset + 1]) {
00969         case T_INT:
00970             full_info = MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_Integer, 1, 0); break;
00971         case T_LONG:
00972         full_info = MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_Long, 1, 0); break;
00973         case T_FLOAT:
00974                 full_info = MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_Float, 1, 0); break;
00975         case T_DOUBLE:
00976         full_info = MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_Double, 1, 0); break;
00977         case T_BYTE:
00978         full_info = MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_Byte, 1, 0); break;
00979         case T_BOOLEAN:
00980         full_info = MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_Boolean, 1, 0); break;
00981         case T_CHAR:
00982         full_info = MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_Char, 1, 0); break;
00983         case T_SHORT:
00984         full_info = MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_Short, 1, 0); break;
00985         default:
00986                 full_info = 0;  /* make GCC happy */
00987         CCerror(context, "Bad type passed to opc_newarray");
00988     }
00989     this_idata->operand.fi = full_info;
00990     break;
00991     }
00992 
00993     /* Fudge iload_x, aload_x, etc to look like their generic cousin. */
00994     case opc_iload_0: case opc_iload_1: case opc_iload_2: case opc_iload_3:
00995     this_idata->opcode = opc_iload;
00996     var = opcode - opc_iload_0;
00997     goto check_local_variable;
00998 
00999     case opc_fload_0: case opc_fload_1: case opc_fload_2: case opc_fload_3:
01000     this_idata->opcode = opc_fload;
01001     var = opcode - opc_fload_0;
01002     goto check_local_variable;
01003 
01004     case opc_aload_0: case opc_aload_1: case opc_aload_2: case opc_aload_3:
01005     this_idata->opcode = opc_aload;
01006     var = opcode - opc_aload_0;
01007     goto check_local_variable;
01008 
01009     case opc_lload_0: case opc_lload_1: case opc_lload_2: case opc_lload_3:
01010     this_idata->opcode = opc_lload;
01011     var = opcode - opc_lload_0;
01012     goto check_local_variable2;
01013 
01014     case opc_dload_0: case opc_dload_1: case opc_dload_2: case opc_dload_3:
01015     this_idata->opcode = opc_dload;
01016     var = opcode - opc_dload_0;
01017     goto check_local_variable2;
01018 
01019     case opc_istore_0: case opc_istore_1: case opc_istore_2: case opc_istore_3:
01020     this_idata->opcode = opc_istore;
01021     var = opcode - opc_istore_0;
01022     goto check_local_variable;
01023 
01024     case opc_fstore_0: case opc_fstore_1: case opc_fstore_2: case opc_fstore_3:
01025     this_idata->opcode = opc_fstore;
01026     var = opcode - opc_fstore_0;
01027     goto check_local_variable;
01028 
01029     case opc_astore_0: case opc_astore_1: case opc_astore_2: case opc_astore_3:
01030     this_idata->opcode = opc_astore;
01031     var = opcode - opc_astore_0;
01032     goto check_local_variable;
01033 
01034     case opc_lstore_0: case opc_lstore_1: case opc_lstore_2: case opc_lstore_3:
01035     this_idata->opcode = opc_lstore;
01036     var = opcode - opc_lstore_0;
01037     goto check_local_variable2;
01038 
01039     case opc_dstore_0: case opc_dstore_1: case opc_dstore_2: case opc_dstore_3:
01040     this_idata->opcode = opc_dstore;
01041     var = opcode - opc_dstore_0;
01042     goto check_local_variable2;
01043 
01044     case opc_wide:
01045     this_idata->opcode = code[offset + 1];
01046     var = (code[offset + 2] << 8) + code[offset + 3];
01047     switch(this_idata->opcode) {
01048         case opc_lload:  case opc_dload:
01049         case opc_lstore: case opc_dstore:
01050             goto check_local_variable2;
01051         default:
01052             goto check_local_variable;
01053     }
01054 
01055     case opc_iinc:        /* the increment amount doesn't matter */
01056     case opc_ret:
01057     case opc_aload: case opc_iload: case opc_fload:
01058     case opc_astore: case opc_istore: case opc_fstore:
01059     var = code[offset + 1];
01060     check_local_variable:
01061     /* Make sure that the variable number isn't illegal. */
01062     this_idata->operand.i = var;
01063     if (var >= (int)mb->nlocals)
01064         CCerror(context, "Illegal local variable number");
01065     break;
01066 
01067     case opc_lload: case opc_dload: case opc_lstore: case opc_dstore:
01068     var = code[offset + 1];
01069     check_local_variable2:
01070     /* Make sure that the variable number isn't illegal. */
01071     this_idata->operand.i = var;
01072     if ((var + 1) >= (int)mb->nlocals)
01073         CCerror(context, "Illegal local variable number");
01074     break;
01075 
01076     default:
01077     if (opcode >= opc_breakpoint)
01078         CCerror(context, "Quick instructions shouldn't appear yet.");
01079     break;
01080     } /* of switch */
01081 }
01082 
01083 /*=========================================================================
01084  * FUNCTION:      set_protected
01085  * OVERVIEW:      Checks the field access to see if the instruction is
01086  *                protected, is private and is in the same class package,
01087  *                then the protected bit for the given instruction is set.
01088  *                Invoked by verify_operands_opcodes() to set protected bit
01089  *                for instructions of the following opcode types:
01090  *                opc_getfield, opc_putfield, and opc_invokevirtual.
01091  * INTERFACE:
01092  *   parameters:  context_type *: context
01093  *                int: instruction number
01094  *                int: key
01095  *                opcode_type: opcode
01096  *
01097  *   returns:     nothing
01098  *=======================================================================*/
01099 static void
01100 set_protected(context_type *context, int inumber, int key, opcode_type opcode)
01101 {
01102     fullinfo_type clazz_info = cp_index_to_class_fullinfo(context, key, true);
01103     if (isSuperClass(context, clazz_info)) {
01104     char *name = cp_index_to_fieldname(context, key);
01105     char *signature = cp_index_to_signature(context, key);
01106     unsigned ID = NameAndTypeToHash(name, signature);
01107     ClassClass *calledClass =
01108         object_fullinfo_to_classclass(context, clazz_info);
01109     struct fieldblock *fb;
01110 
01111     if (ID == 0) {    /* NameAndTypeToHash returns 0 if out of memory */
01112             CCerror(context, "Out of memory");
01113             return;
01114         }
01115     if (opcode != opc_invokevirtual && opcode != opc_invokespecial) {
01116         int n = cbFieldsCount(calledClass);
01117         fb = cbFields(calledClass);
01118         for (; --n >= 0; fb++) {
01119         if (fb->ID == ID) {
01120             goto haveIt;
01121         }
01122         }
01123         return;
01124     } else {
01125         struct methodblock *mb = cbMethods(calledClass);
01126         int n = cbMethodsCount(calledClass);
01127         for (; --n >= 0; mb++) {
01128         if (mb->fb.ID == ID) {
01129             fb = &mb->fb;
01130             goto haveIt;
01131         }
01132         }
01133         return;
01134     }
01135     haveIt:
01136     if (IsProtected(fb->access)) {
01137         if (IsPrivate(fb->access) ||
01138             !IsSameClassPackage(calledClass, context->class))
01139         context->instruction_data[inumber].protected = true;
01140     }
01141     }
01142 }
01143 
01144 /*=========================================================================
01145  * FUNCTION:      isSuperClass
01146  * OVERVIEW:      Determines which of the classes are superclasses.
01147  *                Returns true if the given clazz_info corresponds to a
01148  *                a superclass.
01149  * INTERFACE:
01150  *   parameters:  context_type* : context
01151  *                fullinfo_type: clazz_info
01152  *
01153  *   returns:     boolean type
01154  *=======================================================================*/
01155 static bool
01156 isSuperClass(context_type *context, fullinfo_type clazz_info) {
01157     fullinfo_type *fptr = context->superClasses;
01158     if (fptr == NULL) {
01159     ClassClass *cb;
01160     fullinfo_type *gptr;
01161     int i;
01162     /* Count the number of superclasses.  By counting ourselves, and
01163      * not counting Object, we get the same number.    */
01164     for (i = 0, cb = context->class;
01165          cb != classJavaLangObject;
01166          i++, cb = cbSuperclass(cb));
01167     /* Can't go on context heap since it survives more than one method */
01168     context->superClasses = fptr
01169         = sysMalloc(sizeof(fullinfo_type)*(i + 1));
01170     if (fptr == 0) {
01171         CCerror(context, "Out of memory");
01172     }
01173     for (gptr = fptr, cb = context->class; cb != classJavaLangObject; ) {
01174         void **addr;
01175         cb = cbSuperclass(cb);
01176         *gptr++ = MAKE_CLASSNAME_INFO_WITH_COPY(context, cbName(cb), &addr);
01177         *addr = cb;
01178     }
01179     *gptr = 0;
01180     }
01181     for (; *fptr != 0; fptr++) {
01182     if (*fptr == clazz_info)
01183         return true;
01184     }
01185     return false;
01186 }
01187 
01188 /*=========================================================================
01189  * FUNCTION:      initialize_exception_table
01190  * OVERVIEW:      Initializes the exception table.
01191  *                Looks through each item on the exception table and ensures
01192  *                that each of the fields refers to a legal instruction.
01193  * INTERFACE:
01194  *   parameters:  pointer to the context_type structure.
01195  *
01196  *   returns:     nothing
01197  *=======================================================================*/
01198 static void
01199 initialize_exception_table(context_type *context)
01200 {
01201     struct methodblock *mb = context->mb;
01202     struct CatchFrame *exception_table = mb->exception_table;
01203     struct handler_info_type *handler_info = context->handler_info;
01204     short *code_data = context->code_data;
01205     uint32_t code_length = mb->code_length;
01206 
01207     int i;
01208     for (i = mb->exception_table_length;
01209          --i >= 0; exception_table++, handler_info++) {
01210     uint32_t start = exception_table->start_pc;
01211     uint32_t end = exception_table->end_pc;
01212     uint32_t handler = exception_table->handler_pc;
01213     unsigned catchType = exception_table->catchType;
01214     stack_item_type *stack_item = NEW(stack_item_type, 1);
01215     if (!(  start < end && start >= 0
01216           && isLegalTarget(context, start)
01217           && (end ==  code_length || isLegalTarget(context, end)))) {
01218         CCerror(context, "Illegal exception table range");
01219     }
01220     if (!((handler > 0) && isLegalTarget(context, handler))) {
01221         CCerror(context, "Illegal exception table handler");
01222     }
01223 
01224     handler_info->start = code_data[start];
01225     /* end may point to one byte beyond the end of bytecodes. */
01226     handler_info->end = (end == context->mb->code_length) ?
01227         context->instruction_count : code_data[end];
01228     handler_info->handler = code_data[handler];
01229     handler_info->stack_info.stack = stack_item;
01230     handler_info->stack_info.stack_size = 1;
01231 
01232     stack_item->next = NULL;
01233     if (catchType != 0) {
01234         union cp_item_type *cp = cbConstantPool(context->class);
01235         char *classname;
01236         verify_constant_pool_type(context, catchType, 1 << CONSTANT_Class);
01237         classname = GetClassConstantClassName(cp, catchType);
01238         stack_item->item = MAKE_CLASSNAME_INFO_WITH_COPY(context, classname, 0);
01239     } else {
01240         stack_item->item = context->throwable_info;
01241     }
01242     }
01243 }
01244 
01245 /*=========================================================================
01246  * FUNCTION:      instruction_length
01247  * OVERVIEW:      Given a pointer to an instruction, return its length.
01248  *                Use the table opcode_length[] which is automatically built.
01249  * INTERFACE:
01250  *   parameters:  pointer to the instruction
01251  *
01252  *   returns:     int type
01253  *=======================================================================*/
01254 static int instruction_length(unsigned char *iptr)
01255 {
01256     int instruction = *iptr;
01257     switch (instruction) {
01258         case opc_tableswitch: {
01259         uint32_t *lpc = (uint32_t *) UCALIGN(iptr + 1);
01260         int index = ntohl(lpc[2]) - ntohl(lpc[1]);
01261         if ((index < 0) || (index > 65535)) {
01262         return -1;    /* illegal */
01263         } else {
01264         return (unsigned char *)(&lpc[index + 4]) - iptr;
01265             }
01266     }
01267 
01268     case opc_lookupswitch: {
01269         uint32_t *lpc = (uint32_t *) UCALIGN(iptr + 1);
01270         int npairs = ntohl(lpc[1]);
01271         if (npairs < 0 || npairs >= 8192)
01272         return  -1;
01273         else
01274         return (unsigned char *)(&lpc[2 * (npairs + 1)]) - iptr;
01275     }
01276 
01277     case opc_wide:
01278         switch(iptr[1]) {
01279             case opc_ret:
01280             case opc_iload: case opc_istore:
01281             case opc_fload: case opc_fstore:
01282             case opc_aload: case opc_astore:
01283             case opc_lload: case opc_lstore:
01284             case opc_dload: case opc_dstore:
01285             return 4;
01286         case opc_iinc:
01287             return 6;
01288         default:
01289             return -1;
01290         }
01291 
01292     default: {
01293         /* A length of 0 indicates an error. */
01294         int length = opcode_length[instruction];
01295         return (length <= 0) ? -1 : length;
01296     }
01297     }
01298 }
01299 
01300 /*=========================================================================
01301  * FUNCTION:      isLegalTarget
01302  * OVERVIEW:      Given the target of a branch, make sure that it is a legal
01303  *                target. Returns true if it is a legal target of a branch.
01304  * INTERFACE:
01305  *   parameters:  pointer to the context_type
01306  *                int: offset
01307  *
01308  *   returns:     boolean type
01309  *=======================================================================*/
01310 static bool
01311 isLegalTarget(context_type *context, int offset)
01312 {
01313     struct methodblock *mb = context->mb;
01314     int code_length = mb->code_length;
01315     short *code_data = context->code_data;
01316     return (offset >= 0 && offset < code_length && code_data[offset] >= 0);
01317 }
01318 
01319 /*=========================================================================
01320  * FUNCTION:      verify_constant_pool_type
01321  * OVERVIEW:      Make sure that an element of the constant pool is really
01322  *                of the indicated type.
01323  * INTERFACE:
01324  *   parameters:  pointer to the context_type
01325  *                int: index
01326  *                unsigned: mask
01327  *   returns:     nothing
01328  *=======================================================================*/
01329 static void
01330 verify_constant_pool_type(context_type *context, int index, unsigned mask)
01331 {
01332     ClassClass *cb = context->class;
01333     union cp_item_type *constant_pool = cbConstantPool(cb);
01334     int nconstants = cbConstantPoolCount(cb);
01335     unsigned char *type_table =
01336     constant_pool[CONSTANT_POOL_TYPE_TABLE_INDEX].type;
01337     unsigned type;
01338 
01339     if ((index <= 0) || (index >= nconstants))
01340     CCerror(context, "Illegal constant pool index");
01341 
01342     type = CONSTANT_POOL_TYPE_TABLE_GET_TYPE(type_table, index);
01343     if ((mask & (1 << type)) == 0)
01344     CCerror(context, "Illegal type in constant pool");
01345 }
01346 
01347 
01348 /*=========================================================================
01349  * Functions for Data Flow Analysis
01350  *=======================================================================*/
01351 
01352 /*=========================================================================
01353  * FUNCTION:      initialize_dataflow
01354  * OVERVIEW:      Initialize for data flow analysis.
01355  * INTERFACE:
01356  *   parameters:  pointer to the context_type
01357  *   returns:     nothing
01358  *=======================================================================*/
01359 static void
01360 initialize_dataflow(context_type *context)
01361 {
01362     instruction_data_type *idata = context->instruction_data;
01363     struct methodblock *mb = context->mb;
01364     fullinfo_type *reg_ptr;
01365     fullinfo_type full_info;
01366     char *p;
01367 
01368     /* Initialize the function entry, since we know everything about it. */
01369     idata[0].stack_info.stack_size = 0;
01370     idata[0].stack_info.stack = NULL;
01371     idata[0].register_info.register_count = mb->args_size;
01372     idata[0].register_info.registers = NEW(fullinfo_type, mb->args_size);
01373     idata[0].register_info.mask_count = 0;
01374     idata[0].register_info.masks = NULL;
01375     idata[0].and_flags = 0;    /* nothing needed */
01376     idata[0].or_flags = FLAG_REACHED; /* instruction reached */
01377     reg_ptr = idata[0].register_info.registers;
01378 
01379     if ((mb->fb.access & ACC_STATIC) == 0) {
01380     /* A non static method.  If this is an <init> method, the first
01381      * argument is an uninitialized object.  Otherwise it is an object of
01382      * the given class type.  java.lang.Object.<init> is special since
01383      * we don't call its superclass <init> method.
01384      */
01385     if (strcmp(mb->fb.name, "<init>") == 0
01386             && context->currentclass_info != context->object_info) {
01387         *reg_ptr++ = MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_InitObject, 0, 0);
01388         idata[0].or_flags |= FLAG_NEED_CONSTRUCTOR;
01389     } else {
01390         *reg_ptr++ = context->currentclass_info;
01391     }
01392     }
01393     /* Fill in each of the arguments into the registers. */
01394     for (p = mb->fb.signature + 1; *p != SIGNATURE_ENDFUNC; ) {
01395     char fieldchar = signature_to_fieldtype(context, &p, &full_info);
01396         if (no_floating_point && (fieldchar == 'D' || fieldchar == 'F')) {
01397             CCerror(context, "Floating point arguments not allowed");
01398         }
01399     switch (fieldchar) {
01400         case 'D': case 'L':
01401             *reg_ptr++ = full_info;
01402             *reg_ptr++ = full_info + 1;
01403         break;
01404         default:
01405             *reg_ptr++ = full_info;
01406         break;
01407     }
01408     }
01409     p++;            /* skip over right parenthesis */
01410     if (*p == 'V') {
01411     context->return_type = MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_Void, 0, 0);
01412     } else {
01413     signature_to_fieldtype(context, &p, &full_info);
01414     context->return_type = full_info;
01415     }
01416 
01417     /* Indicate that we need to look at the first instruction. */
01418     idata[0].changed = true;
01419 }
01420 
01421 /*=========================================================================
01422  * FUNCTION:      run_dataflow
01423  * OVERVIEW:      Execute the data flow analysis as long as there are things
01424  *                to change.
01425  * INTERFACE:
01426  *   parameters:  pointer to the context_type
01427  *   returns:     nothing
01428  *=======================================================================*/
01429 static void
01430 run_dataflow(context_type *context) {
01431     struct methodblock *mb = context->mb;
01432     int max_stack_size = mb->maxstack;
01433     instruction_data_type *idata = context->instruction_data;
01434     int icount = context->instruction_count;
01435     bool work_to_do = true;
01436     int inumber;
01437 
01438     /* Run through the loop, until there is nothing left to do. */
01439     while (work_to_do) {
01440     work_to_do = false;
01441     for (inumber = 0; inumber < icount; inumber++) {
01442         instruction_data_type *this_idata = &idata[inumber];
01443         if (this_idata->changed) {
01444         register_info_type new_register_info;
01445         stack_info_type new_stack_info;
01446         flag_type new_and_flags, new_or_flags;
01447 
01448         this_idata->changed = false;
01449         work_to_do = true;
01450 #ifdef DEBUG_VERIFIER
01451         if (verify_verbose) {
01452             int opcode = this_idata->opcode;
01453             char *opname = (opcode == opc_invokeinit) ?
01454                     "invokeinit" : opnames[opcode];
01455             jio_fprintf(stdout, "Instruction %d: ", inumber);
01456             print_stack(context, &this_idata->stack_info);
01457             print_registers(context, &this_idata->register_info);
01458             print_flags(context,
01459                 this_idata->and_flags, this_idata->or_flags);
01460             jio_fprintf(stdout, "  %s(%d)", opname, this_idata->operand.i);
01461             fflush(stdout);
01462         }
01463 #endif
01464         /* Make sure the registers and flags are appropriate */
01465         check_register_values(context, inumber);
01466         check_flags(context, inumber);
01467 
01468         /* Make sure the stack can deal with this instruction */
01469         pop_stack(context, inumber, &new_stack_info);
01470 
01471         /* Update the registers  and flags */
01472         update_registers(context, inumber, &new_register_info);
01473         update_flags(context, inumber, &new_and_flags, &new_or_flags);
01474 
01475         /* Update the stack. */
01476         push_stack(context, inumber, &new_stack_info);
01477 
01478         if (new_stack_info.stack_size > max_stack_size)
01479             CCerror(context, "Stack size too large");
01480 #ifdef DEBUG_VERIFIER
01481         if (verify_verbose) {
01482             jio_fprintf(stdout, "  ");
01483             print_stack(context, &new_stack_info);
01484             print_registers(context, &new_register_info);
01485             print_flags(context, new_and_flags, new_or_flags);
01486             fflush(stdout);
01487         }
01488 #endif
01489         /* Add the new stack and register information to any
01490          * instructions that can follow this instruction.     */
01491         merge_into_successors(context, inumber,
01492                       &new_register_info, &new_stack_info,
01493                       new_and_flags, new_or_flags);
01494         }
01495     }
01496     }
01497 }
01498 
01499 /*=========================================================================
01500  * FUNCTION:      check_register_values
01501  * OVERVIEW:      Make sure that the registers contain a legitimate value
01502  *                for the given instruction.
01503  * INTERFACE:
01504  *   parameters:  pointer to the context_type
01505  *                int inumber
01506  *   returns:     nothing
01507  *=======================================================================*/
01508 static void
01509 check_register_values(context_type *context, int inumber)
01510 {
01511     instruction_data_type *idata = context->instruction_data;
01512     instruction_data_type *this_idata = &idata[inumber];
01513     opcode_type opcode = this_idata->opcode;
01514     int operand = this_idata->operand.i;
01515     int register_count = this_idata->register_info.register_count;
01516     fullinfo_type *registers = this_idata->register_info.registers;
01517     bool double_word = false;    /* default value */
01518     int type;
01519 
01520     switch (opcode) {
01521         default:
01522         return;
01523         case opc_iload: case opc_iinc:
01524         type = ITEM_Integer; break;
01525         case opc_fload:
01526         type = ITEM_Float; break;
01527         case opc_aload:
01528         type = ITEM_Object; break;
01529         case opc_ret:
01530         type = ITEM_ReturnAddress; break;
01531         case opc_lload:
01532         type = ITEM_Long; double_word = true; break;
01533         case opc_dload:
01534         type = ITEM_Double; double_word = true; break;
01535     }
01536     if (!double_word) {
01537     fullinfo_type reg = registers[operand];
01538     /* Make sure we don't have an illegal register or one with wrong type */
01539     if (operand >= register_count) {
01540         CCerror(context,
01541             "Accessing value from uninitialized register %d", operand);
01542     } else if (WITH_ZERO_EXTRA_INFO(reg) == MAKE_FULLINFO(type, 0, 0)) {
01543         /* the register is obviously of the given type */
01544         return;
01545     } else if (GET_INDIRECTION(reg) > 0 && type == ITEM_Object) {
01546         /* address type stuff be used on all arrays */
01547         return;
01548     } else if (GET_ITEM_TYPE(reg) == ITEM_ReturnAddress) {
01549         CCerror(context, "Cannot load return address from register %d",
01550                       operand);
01551         /* alternatively
01552                   (GET_ITEM_TYPE(reg) == ITEM_ReturnAddress)
01553                    && (opcode == opc_iload)
01554            && (type == ITEM_Object || type == ITEM_Integer)
01555            but this never occurs
01556         */
01557     } else if (reg == ITEM_InitObject && type == ITEM_Object) {
01558         return;
01559     } else if (WITH_ZERO_EXTRA_INFO(reg) ==
01560                 MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_NewObject, 0, 0) &&
01561            type == ITEM_Object) {
01562         return;
01563         } else {
01564         CCerror(context, "Register %d contains wrong type", operand);
01565     }
01566     } else {
01567     /* Make sure we don't have an illegal register or one with wrong type */
01568     if ((operand + 1) >= register_count) {
01569         CCerror(context,
01570             "Accessing value from uninitialized register pair %d/%d",
01571             operand, operand+1);
01572     } else {
01573         if ((registers[operand] == MAKE_FULLINFO(type, 0, 0)) &&
01574         (registers[operand + 1] == MAKE_FULLINFO(type + 1, 0, 0))) {
01575         return;
01576         } else {
01577         CCerror(context, "Register pair %d/%d contains wrong type",
01578                 operand, operand+1);
01579         }
01580     }
01581     }
01582 }
01583 
01584 /*=========================================================================
01585  * FUNCTION:      check_flags
01586  * OVERVIEW:      Make sure that the flags contain legitimate values for this
01587  *                instruction.
01588  * INTERFACE:
01589  *   parameters:  pointer to the context_type
01590  *                int: instruction number
01591  *   returns:     nothing
01592  *=======================================================================*/
01593 static void
01594 check_flags(context_type *context, int inumber)
01595 {
01596     instruction_data_type *idata = context->instruction_data;
01597     instruction_data_type *this_idata = &idata[inumber];
01598     opcode_type opcode = this_idata->opcode;
01599     if (opcode == opc_return) {
01600         /* We need a constructor, but we aren't guaranteed it's called */
01601         if ((this_idata->or_flags & FLAG_NEED_CONSTRUCTOR) &&
01602            !(this_idata->and_flags & FLAG_CONSTRUCTED))
01603         CCerror(context, "Constructor must call super() or this()");
01604         /* fall through */
01605     } else if ((opcode == opc_ireturn) || (opcode == opc_lreturn) ||
01606                (opcode == opc_freturn) || (opcode == opc_dreturn) ||
01607                (opcode == opc_areturn)) {
01608         if (this_idata->or_flags & FLAG_NO_RETURN)
01609         /* This method cannot exit normally */
01610         CCerror(context, "Cannot return normally");
01611     }
01612 }
01613 
01614 /*=========================================================================
01615  * FUNCTION:      pop_stack
01616  * OVERVIEW:      Make sure that the top of the stack contains reasonable
01617  *                values for the given instruction. The post-pop values of
01618  *                the stack and its size are returned in *new_stack_info.
01619  * INTERFACE:
01620  *   parameters:  pointer to the context_type
01621  *                int: instruction number
01622  *                stack_info_type: *new_stack_info
01623  *   returns:     nothing
01624  *=======================================================================*/
01625 static void
01626 pop_stack(context_type *context, int inumber, stack_info_type *new_stack_info)
01627 {
01628     instruction_data_type *idata = context->instruction_data;
01629     instruction_data_type *this_idata = &idata[inumber];
01630     opcode_type opcode = this_idata->opcode;
01631     stack_item_type *stack = this_idata->stack_info.stack;
01632     int stack_size = this_idata->stack_info.stack_size;
01633     char *stack_operands, *p;
01634     char buffer[257];        /* for holding manufactured argument lists */
01635     fullinfo_type stack_extra_info_buffer[256]; /* save info popped off stack */
01636     fullinfo_type *stack_extra_info = &stack_extra_info_buffer[256];
01637     fullinfo_type full_info, put_full_info;
01638 
01639     switch(opcode) {
01640         default:
01641         /* For most instructions, we just use a built-in table */
01642         stack_operands = opcode_in_out[opcode][0];
01643         break;
01644 
01645         case opc_putstatic: case opc_putfield: {
01646         /* The top thing on the stack depends on the signature of
01647          * the object.                         */
01648         int operand = this_idata->operand.i;
01649         char *signature = cp_index_to_signature(context, operand);
01650         char *ip = buffer;
01651 #ifdef DEBUG_VERIFIER
01652         if (verify_verbose) {
01653         print_formatted_fieldname(context, operand);
01654         }
01655 #endif
01656         if (opcode == opc_putfield)
01657         *ip++ = 'A';    /* object for putfield */
01658         *ip++ = signature_to_fieldtype(context, &signature, &put_full_info);
01659         *ip = '\0';
01660         stack_operands = buffer;
01661         break;
01662     }
01663 
01664     case opc_invokevirtual: case opc_invokespecial:
01665         case opc_invokeinit:    /* invokespecial call to <init> */
01666     case opc_invokestatic: case opc_invokeinterface: {
01667         /* The top stuff on the stack depends on the method signature */
01668         int operand = this_idata->operand.i;
01669         char *signature = cp_index_to_signature(context, operand);
01670         char *ip = buffer;
01671         char *p;
01672 #ifdef DEBUG_VERIFIER
01673         if (verify_verbose) {
01674         print_formatted_fieldname(context, operand);
01675         }
01676 #endif
01677         if (opcode != opc_invokestatic)
01678         /* First, push the object */
01679         *ip++ = (opcode == opc_invokeinit ? '@' : 'A');
01680         for (p = signature + 1; *p != SIGNATURE_ENDFUNC; ) {
01681         *ip++ = signature_to_fieldtype(context, &p, &full_info);
01682         if (ip >= buffer + sizeof(buffer) - 1)
01683             CCerror(context, "Signature %s has too many arguments",
01684                 signature);
01685         }
01686         *ip = 0;
01687         stack_operands = buffer;
01688         break;
01689     }
01690 
01691     case opc_multianewarray: {
01692         /* Count can't be larger than 255. So can't overflow buffer */
01693         int count = this_idata->operand2.i;    /* number of ints on stack */
01694         memset(buffer, 'I', count);
01695         buffer[count] = '\0';
01696         stack_operands = buffer;
01697         break;
01698     }
01699 
01700     } /* of switch */
01701 
01702     /* Run through the list of operands >>backwards<< */
01703     for (   p = stack_operands + strlen(stack_operands);
01704         p > stack_operands;
01705         stack = stack->next) {
01706     int type = *--p;
01707     fullinfo_type top_type = stack ? stack->item : 0;
01708     int size = (type == 'D' || type == 'L') ? 2 : 1;
01709     *--stack_extra_info = top_type;
01710     if (stack == NULL)
01711         CCerror(context, "Unable to pop operand off an empty stack");
01712     switch (type) {
01713         case 'I':
01714             if (top_type != MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_Integer, 0, 0))
01715             CCerror(context, "Expecting to find integer on stack");
01716         break;
01717 
01718         case 'F':
01719         if (top_type != MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_Float, 0, 0))
01720             CCerror(context, "Expecting to find float on stack");
01721         break;
01722 
01723         case 'A':        /* object or array */
01724         if (   (GET_ITEM_TYPE(top_type) != ITEM_Object)
01725             && (GET_INDIRECTION(top_type) == 0)) {
01726             /* The thing isn't an object or an array.  Let's see if it's
01727              * one of the special cases  */
01728             if (  (WITH_ZERO_EXTRA_INFO(top_type) ==
01729                     MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_ReturnAddress, 0, 0))
01730             && (opcode == opc_astore))
01731             break;
01732             if (   (GET_ITEM_TYPE(top_type) == ITEM_NewObject
01733                 || (GET_ITEM_TYPE(top_type) == ITEM_InitObject))
01734             && (    (opcode == opc_astore)
01735                              || (opcode == opc_aload)
01736                              || (opcode == opc_ifnull)
01737                              || (opcode == opc_ifnonnull)
01738                              || (opcode == opc_if_acmpeq)
01739                              || (opcode == opc_if_acmpne))
01740                            ) {
01741             break;
01742                     }
01743 
01744                     /* The 2nd edition VM of the specification allows field
01745                      * initializations before the superclass initializer,
01746                      * if the field is defined within the current class.
01747                      */
01748                      if (   (GET_ITEM_TYPE(top_type) == ITEM_InitObject)
01749                          && (opcode == opc_putfield)) {
01750 
01751                         int num_fields;
01752                         int key = this_idata->operand.i;
01753                         fullinfo_type clazz_info = cp_index_to_class_fullinfo(
01754                                                             context, key, true);
01755                         char *name = cp_index_to_fieldname(context, key);
01756                         char *signature = cp_index_to_signature(context, key);
01757                         unsigned ID = NameAndTypeToHash(name, signature);
01758 
01759                         ClassClass *calledClass =
01760                              object_fullinfo_to_classclass(context, clazz_info);
01761                         struct fieldblock *fb;
01762                         if (ID == 0) {
01763                             /* NameAndTypeToHash returns 0
01764                              * if out of memory
01765                              */
01766                             CCerror(context, "Out of memory");
01767                             break;
01768                         }
01769                         num_fields = cbFieldsCount(calledClass);
01770                         fb = cbFields(calledClass);
01771                         for (; --num_fields >= 0; fb++) {
01772                             if (fb->ID == ID) {
01773                                 break;
01774                             }
01775                         }
01776 
01777                         if (num_fields != -1) {
01778                             top_type = context->currentclass_info;
01779                             *stack_extra_info = top_type;
01780                             break;
01781                         }
01782                     }
01783             CCerror(context, "Expecting to find object/array on stack");
01784         }
01785         break;
01786 
01787         case '@': {        /* unitialized object, for call to <init> */
01788         int item_type = GET_ITEM_TYPE(top_type);
01789         if (item_type != ITEM_NewObject && item_type != ITEM_InitObject)
01790             CCerror(context,
01791                 "Expecting to find unitialized object on stack");
01792         break;
01793         }
01794 
01795         case 'O':        /* object, not array */
01796         if (WITH_ZERO_EXTRA_INFO(top_type) !=
01797                MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_Object, 0, 0))
01798             CCerror(context, "Expecting to find object on stack");
01799         break;
01800 
01801         case 'a':        /* integer, object, or array */
01802         if (      (top_type != MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_Integer, 0, 0))
01803                && (GET_ITEM_TYPE(top_type) != ITEM_Object)
01804                && (GET_INDIRECTION(top_type) == 0))
01805             CCerror(context,
01806                 "Expecting to find object, array, or int on stack");
01807         break;
01808 
01809         case 'D':        /* double */
01810         if (top_type != MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_Double, 0, 0))
01811             CCerror(context, "Expecting to find double on stack");
01812         break;
01813 
01814         case 'L':        /* long */
01815         if (top_type != MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_Long, 0, 0))
01816             CCerror(context, "Expecting to find long on stack");
01817         break;
01818 
01819         case ']':        /* array of some type */
01820         if (top_type == NULL_FULLINFO) {
01821             /* do nothing */
01822         } else switch(p[-1]) {
01823             case 'I':    /* array of integers */
01824                 if (top_type != MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_Integer, 1, 0) &&
01825                 top_type != NULL_FULLINFO)
01826                 CCerror(context,
01827                     "Expecting to find array of ints on stack");
01828             break;
01829 
01830             case 'L':    /* array of longs */
01831                 if (top_type != MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_Long, 1, 0))
01832                 CCerror(context,
01833                    "Expecting to find array of longs on stack");
01834             break;
01835 
01836             case 'F':    /* array of floats */
01837                 if (top_type != MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_Float, 1, 0))
01838                 CCerror(context,
01839                  "Expecting to find array of floats on stack");
01840             break;
01841 
01842             case 'D':    /* array of doubles */
01843                 if (top_type != MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_Double, 1, 0))
01844                 CCerror(context,
01845                 "Expecting to find array of doubles on stack");
01846             break;
01847 
01848             case 'A': {    /* array of addresses (arrays or objects) */
01849             int indirection = GET_INDIRECTION(top_type);
01850             if ((indirection == 0) ||
01851                 ((indirection == 1) &&
01852                     (GET_ITEM_TYPE(top_type) != ITEM_Object)))
01853                 CCerror(context,
01854                 "Expecting to find array of objects or arrays "
01855                     "on stack");
01856             break;
01857             }
01858 
01859             case 'b':    /* array of bytes or boolean */
01860                 if (top_type != MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_Byte, 1, 0) &&
01861                 top_type != MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_Boolean, 1, 0))
01862                 CCerror(context,
01863                   "Expecting to find array of bytes or booleans on stack");
01864             break;
01865 
01866             case 'B':    /* array of bytes */
01867                 if (top_type != MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_Byte, 1, 0))
01868                 CCerror(context,
01869                   "Expecting to find array of bytes on stack");
01870             break;
01871 
01872             case 'Z':    /* array of boolean */
01873                 if (top_type != MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_Boolean, 1, 0))
01874                 CCerror(context,
01875                   "Expecting to find array of bytes on stack");
01876             break;
01877 
01878             case 'C':    /* array of characters */
01879                 if (top_type != MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_Char, 1, 0))
01880                 CCerror(context,
01881                   "Expecting to find array of chars on stack");
01882             break;
01883 
01884             case 'S':    /* array of shorts */
01885                 if (top_type != MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_Short, 1, 0))
01886                 CCerror(context,
01887                  "Expecting to find array of shorts on stack");
01888             break;
01889 
01890             case '?':    /* any type of array is okay */
01891                 if (GET_INDIRECTION(top_type) == 0)
01892                 CCerror(context,
01893                     "Expecting to find array on stack");
01894             break;
01895 
01896             default:
01897             CCerror(context, "Internal error #1");
01898             sysAssert(FALSE);
01899             break;
01900         }
01901         p -= 2;        /* skip over [ <char> */
01902         break;
01903 
01904         case '1': case '2': case '3': case '4': /* stack swapping */
01905         if (top_type == MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_Double, 0, 0)
01906             || top_type == MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_Long, 0, 0)) {
01907             if ((p > stack_operands) && (p[-1] == '+')) {
01908             context->swap_table[type - '1'] = top_type + 1;
01909             context->swap_table[p[-2] - '1'] = top_type;
01910             size = 2;
01911             p -= 2;
01912             } else {
01913             CCerror(context,
01914                 "Attempt to split long or double on the stack");
01915             }
01916         } else {
01917             context->swap_table[type - '1'] = stack->item;
01918             if ((p > stack_operands) && (p[-1] == '+'))
01919             p--;    /* ignore */
01920         }
01921         break;
01922         case '+':        /* these should have been caught. */
01923         default:
01924         CCerror(context, "Internal error #2");
01925         sysAssert(FALSE);
01926     }
01927     stack_size -= size;
01928     }
01929 
01930     /* For many of the opcodes that had an "A" in their field, we really
01931      * need to go back and do a little bit more accurate testing.  We can, of
01932      * course, assume that the minimal type checking has already been done.
01933      */
01934     switch (opcode) {
01935     default: break;
01936     case opc_aastore: {    /* array index object  */
01937         fullinfo_type array_type = stack_extra_info[0];
01938         fullinfo_type object_type = stack_extra_info[2];
01939         fullinfo_type target_type = decrement_indirection(array_type);
01940             if (array_type == NULL_FULLINFO) {
01941                break;
01942             }
01943         if ((WITH_ZERO_EXTRA_INFO(target_type) ==
01944                      MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_Object, 0, 0)) &&
01945          (WITH_ZERO_EXTRA_INFO(object_type) ==
01946                      MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_Object, 0, 0))) {
01947         /* I disagree.  But other's seem to think that we should allow
01948          * an assignment of any Object to any array of any Object type.
01949          * There will be an runtime error if the types are wrong.
01950          */
01951         break;
01952         }
01953         if (!isAssignableTo(context, object_type, target_type))
01954         CCerror(context, "Incompatible types for storing into array of "
01955                         "arrays or objects");
01956         break;
01957     }
01958
