01959     case opc_putfield:
01960     case opc_getfield:
01961         case opc_putstatic: {
01962         int operand = this_idata->operand.i;
01963         fullinfo_type stack_object = stack_extra_info[0];
01964         if (opcode == opc_putfield || opcode == opc_getfield) {
01965         if (!isAssignableTo(context,
01966                     stack_object,
01967                     cp_index_to_class_fullinfo(context, operand,
01968                                    true))) {
01969             CCerror(context,
01970                 "Incompatible type for getting or setting field");
01971         }
01972                 /* FY:  Why is this commented out?? */
01973         /*
01974         if (this_idata->protected &&
01975             !isAssignableTo(context, stack_object,
01976                     context->currentclass_info)) {
01977             CCerror(context, "Bad access to protected data");
01978         }*/
01979         }
01980         if (opcode == opc_putfield || opcode == opc_putstatic) {
01981         int item = (opcode == opc_putfield ? 1 : 0);
01982         if (!isAssignableTo(context,
01983                     stack_extra_info[item], put_full_info)) {
01984             CCerror(context, "Bad type in putfield/putstatic");
01985         }
01986         }
01987         break;
01988     }
01989 
01990         case opc_athrow:
01991         if (!isAssignableTo(context, stack_extra_info[0],
01992                 context->throwable_info)) {
01993         CCerror(context, "Can only throw Throwable objects");
01994         }
01995         break;
01996 
01997     case opc_aaload: {    /* array index */
01998         /* We need to pass the information to the stack updater */
01999         fullinfo_type array_type = stack_extra_info[0];
02000         context->swap_table[0] = decrement_indirection(array_type);
02001         break;
02002     }
02003 
02004         case opc_invokevirtual: case opc_invokespecial:
02005         case opc_invokeinit:
02006     case opc_invokeinterface: case opc_invokestatic: {
02007         int operand = this_idata->operand.i;
02008         char *signature = cp_index_to_signature(context, operand);
02009         int item;
02010         char *p;
02011         if (opcode == opc_invokestatic) {
02012         item = 0;
02013         } else if (opcode == opc_invokeinit) {
02014         fullinfo_type init_type = this_idata->operand2.fi;
02015         fullinfo_type object_type = stack_extra_info[0];
02016         context->swap_table[0] = object_type; /* save value */
02017         if (GET_ITEM_TYPE(stack_extra_info[0]) == ITEM_NewObject) {
02018             /* We better be calling the appropriate init.  Find the
02019              * inumber of the "opc_new" instruction", and figure
02020              * out what the type really is.
02021              */
02022             int new_inumber = GET_EXTRA_INFO(stack_extra_info[0]);
02023             fullinfo_type target_type = idata[new_inumber].operand2.fi;
02024             context->swap_table[1] = target_type;
02025             if (target_type != init_type) {
02026             CCerror(context, "Call to wrong initialization method");
02027             }
02028         } else {
02029             /* We better be calling super() or this(). */
02030             if (init_type != context->superclass_info &&
02031             init_type != context->currentclass_info) {
02032             CCerror(context, "Call to wrong initialization method");
02033             }
02034             context->swap_table[1] = context->currentclass_info;
02035         }
02036         item = 1;
02037         } else {
02038         fullinfo_type target_type = this_idata->operand2.fi;
02039         fullinfo_type object_type = stack_extra_info[0];
02040         if (!isAssignableTo(context, object_type, target_type)){
02041             CCerror(context,
02042                 "Incompatible object argument for function call");
02043         }
02044                 /* The specification of the structural constraints for
02045                  * invokespecial needs to be more stringent.
02046                  *
02047                  * If invokespecial is used to invoke an instance method
02048                  * that is not an instance initialization method, then the
02049                  * type of the class instance, which is the target of method
02050                  * invocation, must be assignment compatible with the
02051                  * current class.
02052                  */
02053                 if (opcode == opc_invokespecial
02054                     && !isAssignableTo(context, object_type,
02055                                        context->currentclass_info)) {
02056                     /* Make sure object argument is assignment compatible
02057                      * to current class
02058                      */
02059                     CCerror(context,
02060                             "Incompatible object argument for invokespecial");
02061                 }
02062         if (this_idata->protected
02063             && !isAssignableTo(context, object_type,
02064                        context->currentclass_info)) {
02065             /* This is ugly. Special dispensation.  Arrays pretend to
02066                implement public Object clone() even though they don't */
02067             if ((target_type == context->object_info) &&
02068             (GET_INDIRECTION(object_type) > 0) &&
02069             (strcmp(cp_index_to_fieldname(context, this_idata->operand.i),
02070                 "clone") == 0)) {
02071             } else {
02072             CCerror(context, "Bad access to protected data");
02073             }
02074         }
02075         item = 1;
02076         }
02077         for (p = signature + 1; *p != SIGNATURE_ENDFUNC; item++)
02078         if (signature_to_fieldtype(context, &p, &full_info) == 'A') {
02079             if (!isAssignableTo(context,
02080                     stack_extra_info[item], full_info)) {
02081             CCerror(context, "Incompatible argument to function");
02082             }
02083         }
02084 
02085         break;
02086     }
02087 
02088         case opc_return:
02089         if (context->return_type != MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_Void, 0, 0))
02090         CCerror(context, "Wrong return type in function");
02091         break;
02092 
02093     case opc_ireturn: case opc_lreturn: case opc_freturn:
02094         case opc_dreturn: case opc_areturn: {
02095         fullinfo_type target_type = context->return_type;
02096         fullinfo_type object_type = stack_extra_info[0];
02097         if (!isAssignableTo(context, object_type, target_type)) {
02098         CCerror(context, "Wrong return type in function");
02099         }
02100         break;
02101     }
02102 
02103         case opc_new: {
02104         /* Make sure that nothing on the stack already looks like what
02105          * we want to create.  I can't image how this could possibly happen
02106          * but we should test for it anyway, since if it could happen, the
02107          * result would be an unitialized object being able to masquerade
02108          * as an initialized one.
02109          */
02110         stack_item_type *item;
02111         for (item = stack; item != NULL; item = item->next) {
02112         if (item->item == this_idata->operand.fi) {
02113             CCerror(context,
02114                 "Uninitialized object on stack at creating point");
02115         }
02116         }
02117         /* Info for update_registers */
02118         context->swap_table[0] = this_idata->operand.fi;
02119         context->swap_table[1] = MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_Bogus, 0, 0);
02120 
02121         break;
02122     }
02123     }
02124     new_stack_info->stack = stack;
02125     new_stack_info->stack_size = stack_size;
02126 }
02127 
02128 /*=========================================================================
02129  * FUNCTION:      update_registers
02130  * OVERVIEW:      Perform the operation on the registers, and return the
02131  *                updated results in new_register_count_p and new_registers.
02132  *                Note that the instruction has already been determined
02133  *                to be legal.
02134  * INTERFACE:
02135  *   parameters:  pointer to the context_type
02136  *                int: instruction number
02137  *                register_info_type: pointer to new register info
02138  *
02139  *   returns:     nothing
02140  *=======================================================================*/
02141 static void
02142 update_registers(context_type *context, int inumber,
02143          register_info_type *new_register_info)
02144 {
02145     instruction_data_type *idata = context->instruction_data;
02146     instruction_data_type *this_idata = &idata[inumber];
02147     opcode_type opcode = this_idata->opcode;
02148     int operand = this_idata->operand.i;
02149     int register_count = this_idata->register_info.register_count;
02150     fullinfo_type *registers = this_idata->register_info.registers;
02151     stack_item_type *stack = this_idata->stack_info.stack;
02152     int mask_count = this_idata->register_info.mask_count;
02153     mask_type *masks = this_idata->register_info.masks;
02154 
02155     /* Use these as default new values. */
02156     int            new_register_count = register_count;
02157     int            new_mask_count = mask_count;
02158     fullinfo_type *new_registers = registers;
02159     mask_type     *new_masks = masks;
02160 
02161     enum { NONE, SINGLE, DOUBLE } access = NONE;
02162     int i;
02163 
02164     /* Remember, we've already verified the type at the top of the stack. */
02165     switch (opcode) {
02166     default: break;
02167         case opc_istore: case opc_fstore: case opc_astore:
02168         access = SINGLE;
02169         goto continue_store;
02170 
02171         case opc_lstore: case opc_dstore:
02172         access = DOUBLE;
02173         goto continue_store;
02174 
02175     continue_store: {
02176         /* We have a modification to the registers.  Copy them if needed. */
02177         fullinfo_type stack_top_type = stack->item;
02178         int max_operand = operand + ((access == DOUBLE) ? 1 : 0);
02179 
02180         if (     max_operand < register_count
02181           && registers[operand] == stack_top_type
02182           && ((access == SINGLE) ||
02183                  (registers[operand + 1]== stack_top_type + 1)))
02184         /* No changes have been made to the registers. */
02185         break;
02186         new_register_count = MAX(max_operand + 1, register_count);
02187         new_registers = NEW(fullinfo_type, new_register_count);
02188         for (i = 0; i < register_count; i++)
02189         new_registers[i] = registers[i];
02190         for (i = register_count; i < new_register_count; i++)
02191         new_registers[i] = MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_Bogus, 0, 0);
02192         new_registers[operand] = stack_top_type;
02193         if (access == DOUBLE)
02194         new_registers[operand + 1] = stack_top_type + 1;
02195         break;
02196     }
02197 
02198         case opc_iload: case opc_fload: case opc_aload:
02199         case opc_iinc: case opc_ret:
02200         access = SINGLE;
02201         break;
02202 
02203         case opc_lload: case opc_dload:
02204         access = DOUBLE;
02205         break;
02206 
02207         case opc_jsr: case opc_jsr_w:
02208         for (i = 0; i < new_mask_count; i++)
02209         if (new_masks[i].entry == operand)
02210             CCerror(context, "Recursive call to jsr entry");
02211             if (context->redoJsr
02212                    && this_idata->operand2.i == UNKNOWN_RET_INSTRUCTION) {
02213                 /* Do nothing */
02214             } else {
02215                 new_masks = add_to_masks(context, masks, mask_count, operand);
02216                 new_mask_count++;
02217             }
02218         break;
02219 
02220         case opc_invokeinit:
02221         case opc_new: {
02222         /* For invokeinit, an uninitialized object has been initialized.
02223          * For new, all previous occurrences of an uninitialized object
02224          * from the same instruction must be made bogus.
02225          * We find all occurrences of swap_table[0] in the registers, and
02226          * replace them with swap_table[1];
02227          */
02228         fullinfo_type from = context->swap_table[0];
02229         fullinfo_type to = context->swap_table[1];
02230 
02231         int i;
02232         for (i = 0; i < register_count; i++) {
02233         if (new_registers[i] == from) {
02234             /* Found a match */
02235             break;
02236         }
02237         }
02238         if (i < register_count) { /* We broke out loop for match */
02239         /* We have to change registers, and possibly a mask */
02240         bool copied_mask = false;
02241         int k;
02242         new_registers = NEW(fullinfo_type, register_count);
02243         memcpy(new_registers, registers,
02244                register_count * sizeof(registers[0]));
02245         for ( ; i < register_count; i++) {
02246             if (new_registers[i] == from) {
02247             new_registers[i] = to;
02248             for (k = 0; k < new_mask_count; k++) {
02249                 if (!IS_BIT_SET(new_masks[k].modifies, i)) {
02250                 if (!copied_mask) {
02251                     new_masks = copy_masks(context, new_masks,
02252                                mask_count);
02253                     copied_mask = true;
02254                 }
02255                 SET_BIT(new_masks[k].modifies, i);
02256                 }
02257             }
02258             }
02259         }
02260         }
02261         break;
02262     }
02263     } /* of switch */
02264 
02265     if ((access != NONE) && (new_mask_count > 0)) {
02266     int i, j;
02267     for (i = 0; i < new_mask_count; i++) {
02268         int *mask = new_masks[i].modifies;
02269         if ((!IS_BIT_SET(mask, operand)) ||
02270           ((access == DOUBLE) && !IS_BIT_SET(mask, operand + 1))) {
02271         new_masks = copy_masks(context, new_masks, mask_count);
02272         for (j = i; j < new_mask_count; j++) {
02273             SET_BIT(new_masks[j].modifies, operand);
02274             if (access == DOUBLE)
02275             SET_BIT(new_masks[j].modifies, operand + 1);
02276         }
02277         break;
02278         }
02279     }
02280     }
02281 
02282     new_register_info->register_count = new_register_count;
02283     new_register_info->registers = new_registers;
02284     new_register_info->masks = new_masks;
02285     new_register_info->mask_count = new_mask_count;
02286 }
02287 
02288 /*=========================================================================
02289  * FUNCTION:      update_flags
02290  * OVERVIEW:      Update the flags now that we have already determined
02291  *                the instruction to be legal and have already updated the
02292  *                registers.
02293  * INTERFACE:
02294  *   parameters:  pointer to the context_type
02295  *                int: instruction number
02296  *                flag_type: pointer to new_and_flags
02297  *                flag_type: pointer to new_or_flags
02298  *
02299  *   returns:     nothing
02300  *=======================================================================*/
02301 static void
02302 update_flags(context_type *context, int inumber,
02303          flag_type *new_and_flags, flag_type *new_or_flags)
02304 
02305 {
02306     instruction_data_type *idata = context->instruction_data;
02307     instruction_data_type *this_idata = &idata[inumber];
02308     flag_type and_flags = this_idata->and_flags;
02309     flag_type or_flags = this_idata->or_flags;
02310 
02311     /* Set the "we've done a constructor" flag */
02312     if (this_idata->opcode == opc_invokeinit) {
02313     fullinfo_type from = context->swap_table[0];
02314     if (from == MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_InitObject, 0, 0))
02315         and_flags |= FLAG_CONSTRUCTED;
02316     }
02317     *new_and_flags = and_flags;
02318     *new_or_flags = or_flags;
02319 }
02320 
02321 /*=========================================================================
02322  * FUNCTION:      push_stack
02323  * OVERVIEW:      Perform the operation on the stack.
02324  *                Note that the instruction has already been determined
02325  *                to be legal.
02326  *                new_stack_size_p and new_stack_p point to the results after
02327  *                the pops have already been done. Do the pushes, and then
02328  *                put the results back there.
02329  * INTERFACE:
02330  *   parameters:  pointer to the context_type
02331  *                int: instruction number
02332  *                stack_info_type: pointer to new stack info
02333  *
02334  *   returns:     nothing
02335  *=======================================================================*/
02336 static void
02337 push_stack(context_type *context, int inumber, stack_info_type *new_stack_info)
02338 {
02339     instruction_data_type *idata = context->instruction_data;
02340     instruction_data_type *this_idata = &idata[inumber];
02341     opcode_type opcode = this_idata->opcode;
02342     int operand = this_idata->operand.i;
02343 
02344     int stack_size = new_stack_info->stack_size;
02345     stack_item_type *stack = new_stack_info->stack;
02346     char *stack_results = NULL;
02347 
02348     fullinfo_type full_info = 0;
02349     char buffer[5], *p;        /* actually [2] is big enough */
02350 
02351     /* We need to look at all those opcodes in which either we can't tell the
02352      * value pushed onto the stack from the opcode, or in which the value
02353      * pushed onto the stack is an object or array.  For the latter, we need
02354      * to make sure that full_info is set to the right value.
02355      */
02356     switch(opcode) {
02357         default:
02358         stack_results = opcode_in_out[opcode][1];
02359         break;
02360 
02361     case opc_ldc: case opc_ldc_w: case opc_ldc2_w: {
02362         /* Look to constant pool to determine correct result. */
02363         union cp_item_type *cp = cbConstantPool(context->class);
02364         unsigned char *type_table = cp[CONSTANT_POOL_TYPE_TABLE_INDEX].type;
02365         switch (CONSTANT_POOL_TYPE_TABLE_GET_TYPE(type_table, operand)) {
02366         case CONSTANT_Integer:
02367             stack_results = "I"; break;
02368         case CONSTANT_Float:
02369             stack_results = "F"; break;
02370         case CONSTANT_Double:
02371             stack_results = "D"; break;
02372         case CONSTANT_Long:
02373             stack_results = "L"; break;
02374         case CONSTANT_String:
02375             stack_results = "A";
02376             full_info = context->string_info;
02377             break;
02378         default:
02379             CCerror(context, "Internal error #3");
02380             sysAssert(FALSE);
02381         }
02382         break;
02383     }
02384 
02385         case opc_getstatic: case opc_getfield: {
02386         /* Look to signature to determine correct result. */
02387         int operand = this_idata->operand.i;
02388         char *signature = cp_index_to_signature(context, operand);
02389 #ifdef DEBUG_VERIFIER
02390         if (verify_verbose) {
02391         print_formatted_fieldname(context, operand);
02392         }
02393 #endif
02394         buffer[0] = signature_to_fieldtype(context, &signature, &full_info);
02395         buffer[1] = '\0';
02396         stack_results = buffer;
02397         break;
02398     }
02399 
02400     case opc_invokevirtual: case opc_invokespecial:
02401         case opc_invokeinit:
02402     case opc_invokestatic: case opc_invokeinterface: {
02403         /* Look to signature to determine correct result. */
02404         int operand = this_idata->operand.i;
02405         char *signature = cp_index_to_signature(context, operand);
02406         char *result_signature = strchr(signature, SIGNATURE_ENDFUNC) + 1;
02407         if (result_signature[0] == SIGNATURE_VOID) {
02408         stack_results = "";
02409         } else {
02410         buffer[0] = signature_to_fieldtype(context, &result_signature,
02411                            &full_info);
02412         buffer[1] = '\0';
02413         stack_results = buffer;
02414         }
02415         break;
02416     }
02417 
02418     case opc_aconst_null:
02419         stack_results = opcode_in_out[opcode][1];
02420         full_info = NULL_FULLINFO; /* special NULL */
02421         break;
02422 
02423     case opc_new:
02424     case opc_checkcast:
02425     case opc_newarray:
02426     case opc_anewarray:
02427         case opc_multianewarray:
02428         stack_results = opcode_in_out[opcode][1];
02429         /* Conventionally, this result type is stored here */
02430         full_info = this_idata->operand.fi;
02431             if (no_floating_point && (opcode == opc_newarray)
02432                 && (full_info == MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_Float, 1, 0) ||
02433                     full_info == MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_Double, 1, 0))) {
02434                 CCerror(context, "Cannot create floating point array");
02435             }
02436             break;
02437 
02438     case opc_aaload:
02439         stack_results = opcode_in_out[opcode][1];
02440         /* pop_stack() saved value for us. */
02441         full_info = context->swap_table[0];
02442         break;
02443 
02444     case opc_aload:
02445         stack_results = opcode_in_out[opcode][1];
02446         /* The register hasn't been modified, so we can use its value. */
02447         full_info = this_idata->register_info.registers[operand];
02448         break;
02449     } /* of switch */
02450 
02451     for (p = stack_results; *p != 0; p++) {
02452     int type = *p;
02453     stack_item_type *new_item = NEW(stack_item_type, 1);
02454     new_item->next = stack;
02455     stack = new_item;
02456     switch (type) {
02457         case 'I':
02458             stack->item = MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_Integer, 0, 0); break;
02459         case 'F':
02460                 if (no_floating_point) {
02461                     CCerror(context, "Floating point result not allowed");
02462                 }
02463         stack->item = MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_Float, 0, 0);
02464                 break;
02465         case 'D':
02466                 if (no_floating_point) {
02467                     CCerror(context, "Floating point result not allowed");
02468                 }
02469         stack->item = MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_Double, 0, 0);
02470         stack_size++;
02471                 break;
02472         case 'L':
02473         stack->item = MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_Long, 0, 0);
02474         stack_size++; break;
02475         case 'R':
02476         stack->item = MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_ReturnAddress, 0, operand);
02477         break;
02478         case '1': case '2': case '3': case '4': {
02479         /* Get the info saved in the swap_table */
02480         fullinfo_type stype = context->swap_table[type - '1'];
02481         stack->item = stype;
02482         if (stype == MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_Long, 0, 0) ||
02483             stype == MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_Double, 0, 0)) {
02484             stack_size++; p++;
02485         }
02486         break;
02487         }
02488         case 'A':
02489         /* full_info should have the appropriate value. */
02490         sysAssert(full_info != 0);
02491         stack->item = full_info;
02492         break;
02493         default:
02494         CCerror(context, "Internal error #4");
02495         sysAssert(FALSE);
02496 
02497         } /* switch type */
02498     stack_size++;
02499     } /* outer for loop */
02500 
02501     if (opcode == opc_invokeinit) {
02502     /* If there are any instances of "from" on the stack, we need to
02503      * replace it with "to", since calling <init> initializes all versions
02504      * of the object, obviously.     */
02505     fullinfo_type from = context->swap_table[0];
02506     stack_item_type *ptr;
02507     for (ptr = stack; ptr != NULL; ptr = ptr->next) {
02508         if (ptr->item == from) {
02509         fullinfo_type to = context->swap_table[1];
02510         stack = copy_stack(context, stack);
02511         for (ptr = stack; ptr != NULL; ptr = ptr->next)
02512             if (ptr->item == from) ptr->item = to;
02513         break;
02514         }
02515     }
02516     }
02517 
02518     new_stack_info->stack_size = stack_size;
02519     new_stack_info->stack = stack;
02520 }
02521 
02522 /*=========================================================================
02523  * FUNCTION:      merge_into_successors
02524  * OVERVIEW:      Executed an instruction, and have determined the new
02525  *                registers and stack values. Look at all of the possibly
02526  *                subsequent instructions, and merge this stack value into
02527  *                theirs.
02528  * INTERFACE:
02529  *   parameters:  pointer to the context_type
02530  *                int: instruction number
02531  *                stack_info_type: pointer to stack_info
02532  *                flag_type: and_flags
02533  *                flag_type: or_flags
02534  *
02535  *   returns:     nothing
02536  *=======================================================================*/
02537 static void
02538 merge_into_successors(context_type *context, int inumber,
02539               register_info_type *register_info,
02540               stack_info_type *stack_info,
02541               flag_type and_flags, flag_type or_flags)
02542 {
02543     instruction_data_type *idata = context->instruction_data;
02544     instruction_data_type *this_idata = &idata[inumber];
02545     opcode_type opcode = this_idata->opcode;
02546     int operand = this_idata->operand.i;
02547     struct handler_info_type *handler_info = context->handler_info;
02548     int handler_info_length = context->mb->exception_table_length;
02549 
02550     int buffer[2];        /* default value for successors */
02551     int *successors = buffer;    /* table of successors */
02552     int successors_count;
02553     int i;
02554 
02555     switch (opcode) {
02556     default:
02557     successors_count = 1;
02558     buffer[0] = inumber + 1;
02559     break;
02560 
02561     case opc_ifeq: case opc_ifne: case opc_ifgt:
02562     case opc_ifge: case opc_iflt: case opc_ifle:
02563     case opc_ifnull: case opc_ifnonnull:
02564     case opc_if_icmpeq: case opc_if_icmpne: case opc_if_icmpgt:
02565     case opc_if_icmpge: case opc_if_icmplt: case opc_if_icmple:
02566     case opc_if_acmpeq: case opc_if_acmpne:
02567     successors_count = 2;
02568     buffer[0] = inumber + 1;
02569     buffer[1] = operand;
02570         idata[operand].is_target = true; /* inlinejsr */
02571     break;
02572 
02573     case opc_jsr: case opc_jsr_w:
02574     if (this_idata->operand2.i != UNKNOWN_RET_INSTRUCTION)
02575         idata[this_idata->operand2.i].changed = true;
02576     /* FALLTHROUGH */
02577     case opc_goto: case opc_goto_w:
02578     successors_count = 1;
02579     buffer[0] = operand;
02580         idata[operand].is_target = true; /* inlinejsr */
02581     break;
02582 
02583     case opc_ireturn: case opc_lreturn: case opc_return:
02584     case opc_freturn: case opc_dreturn: case opc_areturn:
02585     case opc_athrow:
02586     /* The testing for the returns is handled in pop_stack() */
02587     successors_count = 0;
02588     break;
02589 
02590     case opc_ret: {
02591     /* This is slightly slow, but good enough for a seldom used instruction.
02592      * The EXTRA_ITEM_INFO of the ITEM_ReturnAddress indicates the
02593      * address of the first instruction of the subroutine.  We can return
02594      * to 1 after any instruction that jsr's to that instruction.
02595      */
02596     if (this_idata->operand2.ip == NULL) {
02597         fullinfo_type *registers = this_idata->register_info.registers;
02598         int called_instruction = GET_EXTRA_INFO(registers[operand]);
02599         int i, count, *ptr;;
02600         for (i = context->instruction_count, count = 0; --i >= 0; ) {
02601         if (((idata[i].opcode == opc_jsr) ||
02602              (idata[i].opcode == opc_jsr_w)) &&
02603             (idata[i].operand.i == called_instruction))
02604             count++;
02605         }
02606         this_idata->operand2.ip = ptr = NEW(int, count + 1);
02607         *ptr++ = count;
02608         for (i = context->instruction_count, count = 0; --i >= 0; ) {
02609         if (((idata[i].opcode == opc_jsr) ||
02610              (idata[i].opcode == opc_jsr_w)) &&
02611             (idata[i].operand.i == called_instruction)) {
02612             *ptr++ = i + 1;
02613                     idata[i + 1].is_target = true; /* inlinejsr */
02614                 }
02615         }
02616     }
02617     successors = this_idata->operand2.ip; /* use this instead */
02618     successors_count = *successors++;
02619     break;
02620 
02621     }
02622 
02623     case opc_tableswitch:
02624     case opc_lookupswitch: {
02625         int i;
02626         successors = this_idata->operand.ip; /* use this instead */
02627         successors_count = *successors++;
02628         for (i = 0; i < successors_count; i++) {
02629             idata[successors[i]].is_target = true; /* inlinejsr */
02630         }
02631         break;
02632     }
02633 
02634     }
02635 
02636 #ifdef DEBUG_VERIFIER
02637     if (verify_verbose) {
02638     jio_fprintf(stdout, " [");
02639     for (i = handler_info_length; --i >= 0; handler_info++)
02640         if (handler_info->start <= inumber && handler_info->end > inumber)
02641         jio_fprintf(stdout, "%d* ", handler_info->handler);
02642     for (i = 0; i < successors_count; i++)
02643         jio_fprintf(stdout, "%d ", successors[i]);
02644     jio_fprintf(stdout,   "]\n");
02645     }
02646 #endif
02647 
02648     handler_info = context->handler_info;
02649     for (i = handler_info_length; --i >= 0; handler_info++) {
02650     if (handler_info->start <= inumber && handler_info->end > inumber) {
02651         int handler = handler_info->handler;
02652             idata[handler].is_target = true; /* inlinejsr */
02653         if (opcode != opc_invokeinit) {
02654         merge_into_one_successor(context, inumber, handler,
02655                      &this_idata->register_info, /* old */
02656                      &handler_info->stack_info,
02657                      (flag_type) (and_flags
02658                               & this_idata->and_flags),
02659                      (flag_type) (or_flags
02660                               | this_idata->or_flags),
02661                      true);
02662         } else {
02663         /* We need to be a little bit more careful with this
02664          * instruction.  Things could either be in the state before
02665          * the instruction or in the state afterwards */
02666         fullinfo_type from = context->swap_table[0];
02667         flag_type temp_or_flags = or_flags;
02668         if (from == MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_InitObject, 0, 0))
02669             temp_or_flags |= FLAG_NO_RETURN;
02670         merge_into_one_successor(context, inumber, handler,
02671                      &this_idata->register_info, /* old */
02672                      &handler_info->stack_info,
02673                      this_idata->and_flags,
02674                      this_idata->or_flags,
02675                      true);
02676         merge_into_one_successor(context, inumber, handler,
02677                      register_info,
02678                      &handler_info->stack_info,
02679                      and_flags, temp_or_flags, true);
02680         }
02681     }
02682     }
02683     for (i = 0; i < successors_count; i++) {
02684     int target = successors[i];
02685     if (target >= context->instruction_count)
02686         CCerror(context, "Falling off the end of the code");
02687     merge_into_one_successor(context, inumber, target,
02688                  register_info, stack_info, and_flags, or_flags,
02689                  false);
02690     }
02691 }
02692 
02693 /*=========================================================================
02694  * FUNCTION:      merge_into_one_successor
02695  * OVERVIEW:      Executed an instruction, and have determined a new
02696  *                set of registers and stack values for a given instruction.
02697  *                Merge this new set into the values that are already there.
02698  * INTERFACE:
02699  *   parameters:  pointer to the context_type
02700  *                int: instruction number
02701  *                stack_info_type: pointer to stack_info
02702  *                flag_type: new_and_flags
02703  *                flag_type: new_or_flags
02704  *                boolean type: isException
02705  *
02706  *   returns:     nothing
02707  *=======================================================================*/
02708 static void
02709 merge_into_one_successor(context_type *context,
02710              int from_inumber, int to_inumber,
02711              register_info_type *new_register_info,
02712              stack_info_type *new_stack_info,
02713              flag_type new_and_flags, flag_type new_or_flags,
02714              bool isException)
02715 {
02716     instruction_data_type *idata = context->instruction_data;
02717     register_info_type register_info_buf;
02718     stack_info_type stack_info_buf;
02719 #ifdef DEBUG_VERIFIER
02720     instruction_data_type *this_idata = &idata[to_inumber];
02721     register_info_type old_reg_info;
02722     stack_info_type old_stack_info;
02723     flag_type old_and_flags, old_or_flags;
02724 #endif
02725 
02726 #ifdef DEBUG_VERIFIER
02727     if (verify_verbose) {
02728     old_reg_info = this_idata->register_info;
02729     old_stack_info = this_idata->stack_info;
02730     old_and_flags = this_idata->and_flags;
02731     old_or_flags = this_idata->or_flags;
02732     }
02733 #endif
02734 
02735     /* All uninitialized objects are set to "bogus" when jsr and
02736      * ret are executed. Thus uninitialized objects can't propagate
02737      * into or out of a subroutine.
02738      */
02739     if (idata[from_inumber].opcode == opc_ret ||
02740     idata[from_inumber].opcode == opc_jsr ||
02741     idata[from_inumber].opcode == opc_jsr_w) {
02742     int new_register_count = new_register_info->register_count;
02743     fullinfo_type *new_registers = new_register_info->registers;
02744         int i;
02745     stack_item_type *item;
02746 
02747     for (item = new_stack_info->stack; item != NULL; item = item->next) {
02748         if (GET_ITEM_TYPE(item->item) == ITEM_NewObject) {
02749             /* This check only succeeds for hand-contrived code.
02750          * Efficiency is not an issue.
02751          */
02752             stack_info_buf.stack = copy_stack(context,
02753                           new_stack_info->stack);
02754         stack_info_buf.stack_size = new_stack_info->stack_size;
02755         new_stack_info = &stack_info_buf;
02756         for (item = new_stack_info->stack; item != NULL;
02757              item = item->next) {
02758             if (GET_ITEM_TYPE(item->item) == ITEM_NewObject) {
02759                 item->item = MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_Bogus, 0, 0);
02760             }
02761         }
02762             break;
02763         }
02764     }
02765     for (i = 0; i < new_register_count; i++) {
02766         if (GET_ITEM_TYPE(new_registers[i]) == ITEM_NewObject) {
02767             /* This check only succeeds for hand-contrived code.
02768          * Efficiency is not an issue.
02769          */
02770             fullinfo_type *new_set = NEW(fullinfo_type,
02771                          new_register_count);
02772         for (i = 0; i < new_register_count; i++) {
02773             fullinfo_type t = new_registers[i];
02774             new_set[i] = GET_ITEM_TYPE(t) != ITEM_NewObject ?
02775                 t : MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_Bogus, 0, 0);
02776         }
02777         register_info_buf.register_count = new_register_count;
02778         register_info_buf.registers = new_set;
02779         register_info_buf.mask_count = new_register_info->mask_count;
02780         register_info_buf.masks = new_register_info->masks;
02781         new_register_info = &register_info_buf;
02782         break;
02783         }
02784     }
02785     }
02786 
02787     /* Returning from a subroutine is somewhat ugly.  The actual thing
02788      * that needs to get merged into the new instruction is a joining
02789      * of info from the ret instruction with stuff in the jsr instruction
02790      */
02791     if (idata[from_inumber].opcode == opc_ret && !isException) {
02792     int new_register_count = new_register_info->register_count;
02793     fullinfo_type *new_registers = new_register_info->registers;
02794     int new_mask_count = new_register_info->mask_count;
02795     mask_type *new_masks = new_register_info->masks;
02796     int operand = idata[from_inumber].operand.i;
02797     int called_instruction = GET_EXTRA_INFO(new_registers[operand]);
02798     instruction_data_type *jsr_idata = &idata[to_inumber - 1];
02799     register_info_type *jsr_reginfo = &jsr_idata->register_info;
02800     if (jsr_idata->operand2.i != from_inumber) {
02801         if (jsr_idata->operand2.i != UNKNOWN_RET_INSTRUCTION)
02802         CCerror(context, "Multiple returns to single jsr");
02803         jsr_idata->operand2.i = from_inumber;
02804     }
02805     if (jsr_reginfo->register_count == UNKNOWN_REGISTER_COUNT) {
02806         /* We don't want to handle the returned-to instruction until
02807          * we've dealt with the jsr instruction.   When we get to the
02808          * jsr instruction (if ever), we'll re-mark the ret instruction
02809          */
02810         ;
02811     } else {
02812         int register_count = jsr_reginfo->register_count;
02813         fullinfo_type *registers = jsr_reginfo->registers;
02814         int max_registers = MAX(register_count, new_register_count);
02815         fullinfo_type *new_set = NEW(fullinfo_type, max_registers);
02816         int *return_mask;
02817         struct register_info_type new_new_register_info;
02818         int i;
02819         /* Make sure the place we're returning from is legal! */
02820         for (i = new_mask_count; --i >= 0; )
02821         if (new_masks[i].entry == called_instruction)
02822             break;
02823         if (i < 0)
02824         CCerror(context, "Illegal return from subroutine");
02825         /* pop the masks down to the indicated one.  Remember the mask
02826          * we're popping off. */
02827         return_mask = new_masks[i].modifies;
02828         new_mask_count = i;
02829         for (i = 0; i < max_registers; i++) {
02830         if (IS_BIT_SET(return_mask, i))
02831             new_set[i] = i < new_register_count ?
02832               new_registers[i] : MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_Bogus, 0, 0);
02833         else
02834             new_set[i] = i < register_count ?
02835             registers[i] : MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_Bogus, 0, 0);
02836         }
02837         new_new_register_info.register_count = max_registers;
02838         new_new_register_info.registers      = new_set;
02839         new_new_register_info.mask_count     = new_mask_count;
02840         new_new_register_info.masks          = new_masks;
02841 
02842         merge_stack(context, to_inumber - 1, to_inumber, new_stack_info);
02843         merge_registers(context, to_inumber - 1, to_inumber,
02844                 &new_new_register_info);
02845             /* ADDED FOR JSR_INFO.  Is this correct?? */
02846             merge_flags(context, from_inumber, to_inumber,
02847                         new_and_flags, new_or_flags);
02848     }
02849     } else {
02850     merge_stack(context, from_inumber, to_inumber, new_stack_info);
02851     merge_registers(context, from_inumber, to_inumber, new_register_info);
02852     merge_flags(context, from_inumber, to_inumber,
02853             new_and_flags, new_or_flags);
02854     }
02855 
02856 #ifdef DEBUG_VERIFIER
02857     if (verify_verbose && idata[to_inumber].changed) {
02858     register_info_type *register_info = &this_idata->register_info;
02859     stack_info_type *stack_info = &this_idata->stack_info;
02860     if (memcmp(&old_reg_info, register_info, sizeof(old_reg_info)) ||
02861         memcmp(&old_stack_info, stack_info, sizeof(old_stack_info)) ||
02862         (old_and_flags != this_idata->and_flags) ||
02863         (old_or_flags != this_idata->or_flags)) {
02864         jio_fprintf(stdout, "   %2d:", to_inumber);
02865         print_stack(context, &old_stack_info);
02866         print_registers(context, &old_reg_info);
02867         print_flags(context, old_and_flags, old_or_flags);
02868         jio_fprintf(stdout, " => ");
02869         print_stack(context, &this_idata->stack_info);
02870         print_registers(context, &this_idata->register_info);
02871         print_flags(context, this_idata->and_flags, this_idata->or_flags);
02872         jio_fprintf(stdout, "\n");
02873     }
02874     }
02875 #endif
02876 
02877 }
02878 
02879 /*=========================================================================
02880  * FUNCTION:      merge_stack
02881  * OVERVIEW:      Used by merge_into_one_successor() for merging stack values
02882  *                from a given instruction to another specified instruction.
02883  * INTERFACE:
02884  *   parameters:  pointer to the context_type
02885  *                int: from instruction number
02886  *                int: to instruction number
02887  *                stack_info_type: pointer to new_stack_info
02888  *
02889  *   returns:     nothing
02890  *=======================================================================*/
02891 static void
02892 merge_stack(context_type *context, int from_inumber, int to_inumber,
02893         stack_info_type *new_stack_info)
02894 {
02895     instruction_data_type *idata = context->instruction_data;
02896     instruction_data_type *this_idata = &idata[to_inumber];
02897 
02898     int new_stack_size =  new_stack_info->stack_size;
02899     stack_item_type *new_stack = new_stack_info->stack;
02900 
02901     int stack_size = this_idata->stack_info.stack_size;
02902 
02903     if (stack_size == UNKNOWN_STACK_SIZE) {
02904     /* First time at this instruction.  Just copy. */
02905     this_idata->stack_info.stack_size = new_stack_size;
02906     this_idata->stack_info.stack = new_stack;
02907     this_idata->changed = true;
02908     } else if (new_stack_size != stack_size) {
02909     CCerror(context, "Inconsistent stack height %d != %d",
02910         new_stack_size, stack_size);
02911     } else {
02912     stack_item_type *stack = this_idata->stack_info.stack;
02913     stack_item_type *old, *new;
02914     bool change = false;
02915     for (old = stack, new = new_stack; old != NULL;
02916                old = old->next, new = new->next) {
02917         if (!isAssignableTo(context, new->item, old->item)) {
02918         change = true;
02919         break;
02920         }
02921     }
02922     if (change) {
02923         stack = copy_stack(context, stack);
02924         for (old = stack, new = new_stack; old != NULL;
02925                   old = old->next, new = new->next) {
02926                 if (new == NULL) {
02927                     break;
02928                 }
02929         old->item = merge_fullinfo_types(context, old->item, new->item,
02930                              false);
02931                 if (GET_ITEM_TYPE(old->item) == ITEM_Bogus) {
02932                     CCerror(context, "Mismatched stack types");
02933                 }
02934         }
02935             if (old != NULL || new != NULL) {
02936                 CCerror(context, "Mismatched stack types");
02937             }
02938         this_idata->stack_info.stack = stack;
02939         this_idata->changed = true;
02940     }
02941     }
02942 }
02943 
02944 /*=========================================================================
02945  * FUNCTION:      merge_registers
02946  * OVERVIEW:      Used by merge_into_one_successor() for merging registers
02947  *                from a given instruction to another specified instruction.
02948  * INTERFACE:
02949  *   parameters:  pointer to the context_type
02950  *                int: from instruction number
02951  *                int: to instruction number
02952  *                stack_info_type: pointer to new_stack_info
02953  *
02954  *   returns:     nothing
02955  *=======================================================================*/
02956 static void
02957 merge_registers(context_type *context, int from_inumber, int to_inumber,
02958          register_info_type *new_register_info)
02959 {
02960     instruction_data_type *idata = context->instruction_data;
02961     instruction_data_type *this_idata = &idata[to_inumber];
02962     register_info_type      *this_reginfo = &this_idata->register_info;
02963 
02964     int            new_register_count = new_register_info->register_count;
02965     fullinfo_type *new_registers = new_register_info->registers;
02966     int            new_mask_count = new_register_info->mask_count;
02967     mask_type     *new_masks = new_register_info->masks;
02968 
02969 
02970     if (this_reginfo->register_count == UNKNOWN_REGISTER_COUNT) {
02971     this_reginfo->register_count = new_register_count;
02972     this_reginfo->registers = new_registers;
02973     this_reginfo->mask_count = new_mask_count;
02974     this_reginfo->masks = new_masks;
02975     this_idata->changed = true;
02976     } else {
02977     /* See if we've got new information on the register set. */
02978     int register_count = this_reginfo->register_count;
02979     fullinfo_type *registers = this_reginfo->registers;
02980     int mask_count = this_reginfo->mask_count;
02981     mask_type *masks = this_reginfo->masks;
02982 
02983     bool copy = false;
02984     int i, j;
02985     if (register_count > new_register_count) {
02986         /* Any register larger than new_register_count is now bogus */
02987         this_reginfo->register_count = new_register_count;
02988         register_count = new_register_count;
02989         this_idata->changed = true;
02990     }
02991     for (i = 0; i < register_count; i++) {
02992         fullinfo_type prev_value = registers[i];
02993         if ((i < new_register_count)
02994           ? (!isAssignableTo(context, new_registers[i], prev_value))
02995           : (prev_value != MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_Bogus, 0, 0))) {
02996         copy = true;
02997         break;
02998         }
02999     }
03000 
03001     if (copy) {
03002         /* We need a copy.  So do it. */
03003         fullinfo_type *new_set = NEW(fullinfo_type, register_count);
03004         for (j = 0; j < i; j++)
03005         new_set[j] =  registers[j];
03006         for (j = i; j < register_count; j++) {
03007         if (i >= new_register_count)
03008             new_set[j] = MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_Bogus, 0, 0);
03009         else
03010             new_set[j] = merge_fullinfo_types(context,
03011                               new_registers[j],
03012                               registers[j], false);
03013         }
03014         /* Some of the end items might now be bogus. This step isn't
03015          * necessary, but it may save work later. */
03016         while (   register_count > 0
03017            && GET_ITEM_TYPE(new_set[register_count-1]) == ITEM_Bogus)
03018         register_count--;
03019         this_reginfo->register_count = register_count;
03020         this_reginfo->registers = new_set;
03021         this_idata->changed = true;
03022     }
03023     if (mask_count > 0) {
03024         /* If the target instruction already has a sequence of masks, then
03025          * we need to merge new_masks into it.  We want the entries on
03026          * the mask to be the longest common substring of the two.
03027          *   (e.g.   a->b->d merged with a->c->d should give a->d)
03028          * The bits set in the mask should be the or of the corresponding
03029          * entries in each of the original masks.
03030          */
03031         int i, j, k;
03032         int matches = 0;
03033         int last_match = -1;
03034         bool copy_needed = false;
03035         for (i = 0; i < mask_count; i++) {
03036         int entry = masks[i].entry;
03037         for (j = last_match + 1; j < new_mask_count; j++) {
03038             if (new_masks[j].entry == entry) {
03039             /* We have a match */
03040             int *prev = masks[i].modifies;
03041             int *new = new_masks[j].modifies;
03042             matches++;
03043             /* See if new_mask has bits set for "entry" that
03044              * weren't set for mask.  If so, need to copy. */
03045             for (k = context->bitmask_size - 1;
03046                    !copy_needed && k >= 0;
03047                    k--)
03048                 if (~prev[k] & new[k])
03049                 copy_needed = true;
03050             last_match = j;
03051             break;
03052             }
03053         }
03054         }
03055         if ((matches < mask_count) || copy_needed) {
03056         /* We need to make a copy for the new item, since either the
03057          * size has decreased, or new bits are set. */
03058         mask_type *copy = NEW(mask_type, matches);
03059         for (i = 0; i < matches; i++) {
03060             copy[i].modifies = NEW(int, context->bitmask_size);
03061         }
03062         this_reginfo->masks = copy;
03063         this_reginfo->mask_count = matches;
03064         this_idata->changed = true;
03065         matches = 0;
03066         last_match = -1;
03067         for (i = 0; i < mask_count; i++) {
03068             int entry = masks[i].entry;
03069             for (j = last_match + 1; j < new_mask_count; j++) {
03070             if (new_masks[j].entry == entry) {
03071                 int *prev1 = masks[i].modifies;
03072                 int *prev2 = new_masks[j].modifies;
03073                 int *new = copy[matches].modifies;
03074                 copy[matches].entry = entry;
03075                 for (k = context->bitmask_size - 1; k >= 0; k--)
03076                 new[k] = prev1[k] | prev2[k];
03077                 matches++;
03078                 last_match = j;
03079                 break;
03080             }
03081             }
03082         }
03083         }
03084     }
03085     }
03086 }
03087 
03088 /*=========================================================================
03089  * FUNCTION:      merge_flags
03090  * OVERVIEW:      Used by merge_into_one_successor() for merging flags
03091  *                from a given instruction to a specified instruction.
03092  * INTERFACE:
03093  *   parameters:  pointer to the context_type
03094  *                int: from instruction number
03095  *                int: to instruction number
03096  *                flag_type: new_and_flags
03097  *                flag_type: new_or_flags
03098  *
03099  *   returns:     nothing
03100  *=======================================================================*/
03101 static void
03102 merge_flags(context_type *context, int from_inumber, int to_inumber,
03103         flag_type new_and_flags, flag_type new_or_flags)
03104 {
03105     /* Set this_idata->and_flags &= new_and_flags
03106            this_idata->or_flags |= new_or_flags
03107      */
03108     instruction_data_type *idata = context->instruction_data;
03109     instruction_data_type *this_idata = &idata[to_inumber];
03110     flag_type this_and_flags = this_idata->and_flags;
03111     flag_type this_or_flags = this_idata->or_flags;
03112     flag_type merged_and = this_and_flags & new_and_flags;
03113     flag_type merged_or = this_or_flags | new_or_flags;
03114 
03115     if ((merged_and != this_and_flags) || (merged_or != this_or_flags)) {
03116     this_idata->and_flags = merged_and;
03117     this_idata->or_flags = merged_or;
03118     this_idata->changed = true;
03119     }
03120 }
03121 
03122 /*=========================================================================
03123  * FUNCTION:      copy_stack
03124  * OVERVIEW:      Make a copy of a stack.
03125  * INTERFACE:
03126  *   parameters:  pointer to the context_type
03127  *                stack_item_type: pointer to stack
03128  *
03129  *   returns:     pointer to stack_item_type
03130  *=======================================================================*/
03131 static stack_item_type *
03132 copy_stack(context_type *context, stack_item_type *stack)
03133 {
03134     int length;
03135     stack_item_type *ptr;
03136 
03137     /* Find the length */
03138     for (ptr = stack, length = 0; ptr != NULL; ptr = ptr->next, length++);
03139 
03140     if (length > 0) {
03141     stack_item_type *new_stack = NEW(stack_item_type, length);
03142     stack_item_type *new_ptr;
03143     for (    ptr = stack, new_ptr = new_stack;
03144              ptr != NULL;
03145              ptr = ptr->next, new_ptr++) {
03146         new_ptr->item = ptr->item;
03147         new_ptr->next = new_ptr + 1;
03148     }
03149     new_stack[length - 1].next = NULL;
03150     return new_stack;
03151     } else {
03152     return NULL;
03153     }
03154 }
03155 
03156 /*=========================================================================
03157  * FUNCTION:      copy_masks
03158  * OVERVIEW:      Make a copy of the masks.
03159  * INTERFACE:
03160  *   parameters:  pointer to the context_type
03161  *                mask_type: pointer to masks
03162  *                int: mask count
03163  *
03164  *   returns:     pointer to mask_type
03165  *=======================================================================*/
03166 static mask_type *
03167 copy_masks(context_type *context, mask_type *masks, int mask_count)
03168 {
03169     mask_type *result = NEW(mask_type, mask_count);
03170     int bitmask_size = context->bitmask_size;
03171     int *bitmaps = NEW(int, mask_count * bitmask_size);
03172     int i;
03173     for (i = 0; i < mask_count; i++) {
03174     result[i].entry = masks[i].entry;
03175     result[i].modifies = &bitmaps[i * bitmask_size];
03176     memcpy(result[i].modifies, masks[i].modifies, bitmask_size * sizeof(int));
03177     }
03178     return result;
03179 }
03180 
03181 /*=========================================================================
03182  * FUNCTION:      add_to_masks
03183  * OVERVIEW:      Used by Update_registers for adding entries to masks for
03184  *                JSR instructions.
03185  * INTERFACE:
03186  *   parameters:  pointer to the context_type
03187  *                mask_type: pointer to masks
03188  *                int: mask count
03189  *                int: d
03190  *
03191  *   returns:     pointer to mask_type
03192  *=======================================================================*/
03193 static mask_type *
03194 add_to_masks(context_type *context, mask_type *masks, int mask_count, int d)
03195 {
03196     mask_type *result = NEW(mask_type, mask_count + 1);
03197     int bitmask_size = context->bitmask_size;
03198     int *bitmaps = NEW(int, (mask_count + 1) * bitmask_size);
03199     int i;
03200     for (i = 0; i < mask_count; i++) {
03201     result[i].entry = masks[i].entry;
03202     result[i].modifies = &bitmaps[i * bitmask_size];
03203     memcpy(result[i].modifies, masks[i].modifies, bitmask_size * sizeof(int));
03204     }
03205     result[mask_count].entry = d;
03206     result[mask_count].modifies = &bitmaps[mask_count * bitmask_size];
03207     memset(result[mask_count].modifies, 0, bitmask_size * sizeof(int));
03208     return result;
03209 }
03210 
03211 
03212 /*=========================================================================
03213  * Storage Management Operations
03214  *=======================================================================*/
03215 
03216 /* We create our own storage manager, since we malloc lots of little items,
03217  * and we do not want to keep track of them when they become free.
03218  * It would have been nice if we had heaps, which could all be freed when
03219  * done.
03220  */
03221 
03222 #define CCSegSize 2000
03223 
03224 struct CCpool {            /* a segment of allocated memory in the pool */
03225     struct CCpool *next;
03226     int segSize;        /* almost always CCSegSize */
03227     char space[CCSegSize];
03228 };
03229 
03230 /*=========================================================================
03231  * FUNCTION:      CCinit
03232  * OVERVIEW:      Initialize the context's heap.
03233  * INTERFACE:
03234  *   parameters:  pointer to the context_type
03235  *
03236  *   returns:     nothing
03237  *=======================================================================*/
03238 static void CCinit(context_type *context)
03239 {
03240     struct CCpool *new = (struct CCpool *) sysMalloc(sizeof(struct CCpool));
03241     /* Set context->CCroot to 0 if new == 0 to tell CCdestroy to lay off */
03242     context->CCroot = context->CCcurrent = new;
03243     if (new == 0) {
03244     CCerror(context, "Out of memory");
03245     }
03246     new->next = NULL;
03247     new->segSize = CCSegSize;
03248     context->CCfree_size = CCSegSize;
03249     context->CCfree_ptr = &new->space[0];
03250 }
03251 
03252 /*=========================================================================
03253  * FUNCTION:      CCreinit
03254  * OVERVIEW:      Reuse all the space that we have in the context's heap.
03255  * INTERFACE:
03256  *   parameters:  pointer to the context_type
03257  *
03258  *   returns:     nothing
03259  *=======================================================================*/
03260 static void CCreinit(context_type *context)
03261 {
03262     struct CCpool *first = context->CCroot;
03263     context->CCcurrent = first;
03264     context->CCfree_size = CCSegSize;
03265     context->CCfree_ptr = &first->space[0];
03266 }
03267 
03268 /*=========================================================================
03269  * FUNCTION:      CCdestroy
03270  * OVERVIEW:      Destroy the context's heap.
03271  * INTERFACE:
03272  *   parameters:  pointer to the context_type
03273  *
03274  *   returns:     nothing
03275  *=======================================================================*/
03276 static void CCdestroy(context_type *context)
03277 {
03278     struct CCpool *this = context->CCroot;
03279     while (this) {
03280     struct CCpool *next = this->next;
03281     sysFree(this);
03282     this = next;
03283     }
03284     /* These two aren't necessary.  But can't hurt either */
03285     context->CCroot = context->CCcurrent = NULL;
03286     context->CCfree_ptr = 0;
03287 }
03288 
03289 /*=========================================================================
03290  * FUNCTION:      CCalloc
03291  * OVERVIEW:      Allocate an object of the given size from the context's heap.
03292  * INTERFACE:
03293  *   parameters:  pointer to the context_type
03294  *                int: size
03295  *                bool_t: zero
03296  *
03297  *   returns:     pointer to void
03298  *=======================================================================*/
03299 static void *
03300 CCalloc(context_type *context, int size, bool zero)
03301 {
03302 
03303     register char *p;
03304     /* Round CC to the size of a pointer */
03305     size = (size + (sizeof(void *) - 1)) & ~(sizeof(void *) - 1);
03306 
03307     if (context->CCfree_size <  size) {
03308     struct CCpool *current = context->CCcurrent;
03309     struct CCpool *new;
03310     if (size > CCSegSize) {    /* we need to allocate a special block */
03311         new = (struct CCpool *)sysMalloc(sizeof(struct CCpool) +
03312                          (size - CCSegSize));
03313         if (new == 0) {
03314         CCerror(context, "Out of memory");
03315         }
03316         new->next = current->next;
03317         new->segSize = size;
03318         current->next = new;
03319     } else {
03320         new = current->next;
03321         if (new == NULL) {
03322         new = (struct CCpool *) sysMalloc(sizeof(struct CCpool));
03323         if (new == 0) {
03324             CCerror(context, "Out of memory");
03325         }
03326         current->next = new;
03327         new->next = NULL;
03328         new->segSize = CCSegSize;
03329         }
03330     }
03331     context->CCcurrent = new;
03332     context->CCfree_ptr = &new->space[0];
03333     context->CCfree_size = new->segSize;
03334     }
03335     p = context->CCfree_ptr;
03336     context->CCfree_ptr += size;
03337     context->CCfree_size -= size;
03338     if (zero)
03339     memset(p, 0, size);
03340     return p;
03341 }
03342 
03343 /*=========================================================================
03344  * FUNCTION:      cp_index_to_signature
03345  * OVERVIEW:      Get the signature associated with a particular field or
03346  *                method in the constant pool.
03347  * INTERFACE:
03348  *   parameters:  pointer to the context_type
03349  *                int: constant pool index
03350  *
03351  *   returns:     char * type
03352  *=======================================================================*/
03353 static char *
03354 cp_index_to_signature(context_type *context, int cp_index)
03355 {
03356     union cp_item_type *cp = cbConstantPool(context->class);
03357     int index = cp[cp_index].i; /* value of Fieldref field */
03358     int key2 = index & 0xFFFF;    /* index to NameAndType  */
03359     int signature_index = cp[key2].i & 0xFFFF;
03360     char *signature = cp[signature_index].cp;
03361     return signature;
03362 }
03363 
03364 /*=========================================================================
03365  * FUNCTION:      cp_index_to_fieldname
03366  * OVERVIEW:      Get the fieldname for the specific index within the
03367  *                constant pool.
03368  * INTERFACE:
03369  *   parameters:  pointer to the context_type
03370  *                int: constant pool index
03371  *
03372  *   returns:     char * type
03373  *=======================================================================*/
03374 static char *
03375 cp_index_to_fieldname(context_type *context, int cp_index)
03376 {
03377     union cp_item_type *cp = cbConstantPool(context->class);
03378     int index = cp[cp_index].i; /* value of Fieldref field */
03379     int key2 = index & 0xFFFF;    /* index to NameAndType  */
03380     int name_index = cp[key2].i >> 16;
03381     return cp[name_index].cp;
03382 }
03383 
03384 /*=========================================================================
03385  * FUNCTION:      cp_index_to_class_fullinfo
03386  * OVERVIEW:      Get the class associated with a particular field or
03387  *                method or class in the constant pool. If is_field is true,
03388  *                then it is a field or method. Otherwise, if false, it is a
03389  *                class.
03390  * INTERFACE:
03391  *   parameters:  pointer to the context_type
03392  *                int: constant pool index
03393  *                bool_t: is_field
03394  *
03395  *   returns:     fullinfo_type
03396  *=======================================================================*/
03397 static fullinfo_type
03398 cp_index_to_class_fullinfo(context_type *context, int cp_index, bool is_field)
03399 {
03400     union cp_item_type *cp = cbConstantPool(context->class);
03401     unsigned classkey = is_field ? (cp[cp_index].i >> 16) : cp_index;
03402     char *classname = GetClassConstantClassName(cp, classkey);
03403     if (classname[0] == SIGNATURE_ARRAY) {
03404     fullinfo_type result;
03405     /* This make recursively call us, in case of a class array */
03406     signature_to_fieldtype(context, &classname, &result);
03407     return result;
03408     } else {
03409     return MAKE_CLASSNAME_INFO_WITH_COPY(context, classname, 0);
03410     }
03411 }
03412 
03413 /*=========================================================================
03414  * FUNCTION:      CCerror
03415  * OVERVIEW:      Error handling
03416  * INTERFACE:
03417  *   parameters:  pointer to the context_type
03418  *                char: pointer to format
03419  *
03420  *   returns:     nothing
03421  *=======================================================================*/
03422 static void
03423 CCerror (context_type *context, char *format, ...)
03424 {
03425     va_list args;
03426     struct methodblock *mb = context->mb;
03427     printCurrentClassName();
03428     if (mb != 0) {
03429         jio_fprintf(stderr, "VERIFIER ERROR %s.%s%s:\n",
03430             cbName(fieldclass(&mb->fb)), mb->fb.name, mb->fb.signature);
03431     } else {
03432         jio_fprintf(stderr, "VERIFIER ERROR class %s (mb uninitialized):\n",
03433             cbName(context->class));
03434     }
03435     va_start(args, format);
03436        jio_vfprintf(stderr, format, args);
03437     va_end(args);
03438     jio_fprintf(stderr, "\n");
03439     exit(1);
03440 }
03441 
03442 /*=========================================================================
03443  * FUNCTION:      signature_to_fieldtype
03444  * OVERVIEW:      Given the full info type for a field, returns the field type
03445  *                which corresponds to this signature.
03446  * INTERFACE:
03447  *   parameters:  pointer to the context_type
03448  *                char: **signature_p
03449  *                fullinfo_type: *full_info_p
03450  *
03451  *   returns:     char
03452  *=======================================================================*/
03453 static char
03454 signature_to_fieldtype(context_type *context,
03455                char **signature_p, fullinfo_type *full_info_p)
03456 {
03457     char *p = *signature_p;
03458     fullinfo_type full_info = MAKE_FULLINFO(0, 0, 0);
03459     char result;
03460     int array_depth = 0;
03461 
03462     for (;;) {
03463     switch(*p++) {
03464             default:
03465         full_info = MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_Bogus, 0, 0);
03466         result = 0;
03467         break;
03468 
03469             case SIGNATURE_BYTE:
03470         full_info = (array_depth > 0)
03471                       ? MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_Byte, 0, 0)
03472                       : MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_Integer, 0, 0);
03473         result = 'I';
03474         break;
03475 
03476         case SIGNATURE_BOOLEAN:
03477         full_info = (array_depth > 0)
03478                       ? MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_Boolean, 0, 0)
03479                       : MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_Integer, 0, 0);
03480         result = 'I';
03481         break;
03482 
03483             case SIGNATURE_CHAR:
03484         full_info = (array_depth > 0)
03485                       ? MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_Char, 0, 0)
03486                   : MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_Integer, 0, 0);
03487         result = 'I';
03488         break;
03489 
03490             case SIGNATURE_SHORT:
03491         full_info = (array_depth > 0)
03492                       ? MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_Short, 0, 0)
03493                   : MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_Integer, 0, 0);
03494         result = 'I';
03495         break;
03496 
03497             case SIGNATURE_INT:
03498         full_info = MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_Integer, 0, 0);
03499         result = 'I';
03500         break;
03501 
03502             case SIGNATURE_FLOAT:
03503         full_info = MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_Float, 0, 0);
03504         result = 'F';
03505         break;
03506 
03507             case SIGNATURE_DOUBLE:
03508         full_info = MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_Double, 0, 0);
03509         result = 'D';
03510         break;
03511 
03512         case SIGNATURE_LONG:
03513         full_info = MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_Long, 0, 0);
03514         result = 'L';
03515         break;
03516 
03517             case SIGNATURE_ARRAY:
03518         array_depth++;
03519         continue;    /* only time we ever do the loop > 1 */
03520 
03521             case SIGNATURE_CLASS: {
03522         char buffer_space[256];
03523         char *buffer = buffer_space;
03524         char *finish = strchr(p, SIGNATURE_ENDCLASS);
03525         int length = finish - p;
03526         if (length + 1 > sizeof(buffer_space)) {
03527             buffer = sysMalloc(length + 1);
03528             if (buffer == 0) {
03529             CCerror(context, "Out of memory");
03530             }
03531         }
03532             memcpy(buffer, p, length);
03533         buffer[length] = '\0';
03534         full_info = MAKE_CLASSNAME_INFO_WITH_COPY(context, buffer, 0);
03535         result = 'A';
03536         p = finish + 1;
03537         if (buffer != buffer_space)
03538             sysFree(buffer);
03539         break;
03540         }
03541     } /* end of switch */
03542     break;
03543     }
03544     *signature_p = p;
03545     if (array_depth == 0 || result == 0) {
03546     /* either not an array, or result is bogus */
03547     *full_info_p = full_info;
03548     return result;
03549     } else {
03550     if (array_depth > MAX_ARRAY_DIMENSIONS)
03551         CCerror(context, "Array with too many dimensions");
03552     *full_info_p = MAKE_FULLINFO(GET_ITEM_TYPE(full_info),
03553                      array_depth,
03554                      GET_EXTRA_INFO(full_info));
03555     return 'A';
03556     }
03557 }
03558 
03559 /*=========================================================================
03560  * FUNCTION:      decrement_indirection
03561  * OVERVIEW:      Given an array type, create the type that has one less
03562  *                level of indirection.
03563  * INTERFACE:
03564  *   parameters:  fullinfo_type array_info
03565  *
03566  *   returns:     fullinfo_type
03567  *=======================================================================*/
03568 static fullinfo_type
03569 decrement_indirection(fullinfo_type array_info)
03570 {
03571     if (array_info == NULL_FULLINFO) {
03572     return NULL_FULLINFO;
03573     } else {
03574     int type = GET_ITEM_TYPE(array_info);
03575     int indirection = GET_INDIRECTION(array_info) - 1;
03576     int extra_info = GET_EXTRA_INFO(array_info);
03577     if (   (indirection == 0)
03578            && ((type == ITEM_Short || type == ITEM_Byte || type == ITEM_Boolean || type == ITEM_Char)))
03579         type = ITEM_Integer;
03580     return MAKE_FULLINFO(type, indirection, extra_info);
03581     }
03582 }
03583 
03584 /*=========================================================================
03585  * FUNCTION:      isAssignableTo
03586  * OVERVIEW:      Given an object of the "from" type, determine if it can be
03587  *                assigned to an object of the "to" type.
03588  * INTERFACE:
03589  *   parameters:  pointer to the context_type
03590  *                fullinfo_type: from
03591  *                fullinfo_type: to
03592  *
03593  *   returns:     boolean type
03594  *=======================================================================*/
03595 static bool isAssignableTo(context_type *context,
03596                  fullinfo_type from, fullinfo_type to)
03597 {
03598     return (merge_fullinfo_types(context, from, to, true) == to);
03599 }
03600 
03601 /*=========================================================================
03602  * FUNCTION:      merge_fullinfo_types
03603  * OVERVIEW:      Given two fullinfo_types, find their lowest common deno-
03604  *                minator. If the assignable_p argument is non-null, we are
03605  *                really just calling to find out if "<target> := <value>"
03606  *                is a legitimate assignment.
03607  *                We treat all interfaces as if they were of type '
03608  *                java/lang/Object, since the runtime will do the full
03609  *                checking.
03610  *
03611  * INTERFACE:
03612  *   parameters:  pointer to the context_type
03613  *                fullinfo_type: value
03614  *                fullinfo_type: target
03615  *                bool_t for_assignment
03616  *
03617  *   returns:     fullinfo_type
03618  *=======================================================================*/
03619 static fullinfo_type
03620 merge_fullinfo_types(context_type *context,
03621              fullinfo_type value, fullinfo_type target,
03622              bool for_assignment)
03623 {
03624     if (value == target) {
03625     /* If they're identical, clearly just return what we've got */
03626     return value;
03627     }
03628 
03629     /* Both must be either arrays or objects to go further */
03630     if (GET_INDIRECTION(value) == 0 && GET_ITEM_TYPE(value) != ITEM_Object)
03631     return MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_Bogus, 0, 0);
03632     if (GET_INDIRECTION(target) == 0 && GET_ITEM_TYPE(target) != ITEM_Object)
03633     return MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_Bogus, 0, 0);
03634 
03635     /* If either is NULL, return the other. */
03636     if (value == NULL_FULLINFO)
03637     return target;
03638     else if (target == NULL_FULLINFO)
03639     return value;
03640 
03641     /* If either is java/lang/Object, that's the result. */
03642     if (target == context->object_info)
03643     return target;
03644     else if (value == context->object_info) {
03645     /*   For assignments, Interface := Object, return Interface
03646      * rather than Object, so that isAssignableTo() will get the right
03647      * result.      */
03648     if (for_assignment && (WITH_ZERO_EXTRA_INFO(target) ==
03649                       MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_Object, 0, 0))) {
03650         ClassClass *cb = object_fullinfo_to_classclass(context, target);
03651         if (cb && cbIsInterface(cb))
03652         return target;
03653     }
03654     return value;
03655     }
03656     if (GET_INDIRECTION(value) > 0 || GET_INDIRECTION(target) > 0) {
03657     /* At least one is an array.  Neither is java/lang/Object or NULL.
03658      * Moreover, the types are not identical.
03659      * The result must either be Object, or an array of some object type.
03660      */
03661     int dimen_value = GET_INDIRECTION(value);
03662     int dimen_target = GET_INDIRECTION(target);
03663 
03664     /* First, if either item's base type isn't ITEM_Object, promote it up
03665          * to an object or array of object.  If either is elemental, we can
03666      * punt.
03667          */
03668     if (GET_ITEM_TYPE(value) != ITEM_Object) {
03669         if (dimen_value == 0)
03670         return MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_Bogus, 0, 0);
03671         dimen_value--;
03672         value = MAKE_Object_ARRAY(dimen_value);
03673 
03674     }
03675     if (GET_ITEM_TYPE(target) != ITEM_Object) {
03676         if (dimen_target == 0)
03677         return MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_Bogus, 0, 0);
03678         dimen_target--;
03679         target = MAKE_Object_ARRAY(dimen_target);
03680     }
03681     /* Both are now objects or arrays of some sort of object type */
03682     if (dimen_value == dimen_target) {
03683             /* Arrays of the same dimension.  Merge their base types. */
03684         fullinfo_type value_base = WITH_ZERO_INDIRECTION(value);
03685         fullinfo_type target_base = WITH_ZERO_INDIRECTION(target);
03686         fullinfo_type  result_base =
03687         merge_fullinfo_types(context, value_base, target_base,
03688                         for_assignment);
03689         if (result_base == MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_Bogus, 0, 0))
03690         /* bogus in, bogus out */
03691         return result_base;
03692         return MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_Object, dimen_value,
03693                  GET_EXTRA_INFO(result_base));
03694     } else {
03695             /* Arrays of different sizes.  Return Object, with a dimension
03696              * of the smaller of the two.
03697              */
03698         int dimen = dimen_value < dimen_target ? dimen_value : dimen_target;
03699         return MAKE_Object_ARRAY(dimen);
03700     }
03701     } else {
03702     /* Both are non-array objects. Neither is java/lang/Object or NULL */
03703     ClassClass *cb_value, *cb_target, *cb_super_value, *cb_super_target;
03704     void **addr;
03705     int value_info;
03706 
03707     /* Let's get the classes corresponding to each of these.  Treat
03708      * interfaces as if they were java/lang/Object.  See note above. */
03709     cb_target = object_fullinfo_to_classclass(context, target);
03710     if (cb_target == 0)
03711         return MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_Bogus, 0, 0);
03712     if (cbIsInterface(cb_target))
03713         return for_assignment ? target : context->object_info;
03714     cb_value = object_fullinfo_to_classclass(context, value);
03715     if (cb_value == 0)
03716         return MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_Bogus, 0, 0);
03717     if (cbIsInterface(cb_value))
03718         return context->object_info;
03719 
03720     /* If this is for assignment of target := value, we just need to see if
03721      * cb_target is a superclass of cb_value.  Save ourselves a lot of
03722      * work.
03723      */
03724     if (for_assignment) {
03725         for (cb_super_value = cb_value;
03726          cbSuperclass(cb_super_value) != NULL;
03727          cb_super_value = cbSuperclass(cb_super_value)) {
03728         if (cb_super_value == cb_target) {
03729             return target;
03730         }
03731         }
03732         return context->object_info;
03733     }
03734 
03735     /* Find out whether cb_value or cb_target is deeper in the class
03736      * tree by moving both toward the root, and seeing who gets there
03737      * first.                                                          */
03738     for (cb_super_value = cb_value, cb_super_target = cb_target;
03739          cbSuperclass(cb_super_value) && cbSuperclass(cb_super_target); ) {
03740         /* Optimization.  If either hits the other when going up looking
03741          * for a parent, then might as well return the parent immediately */
03742         if (cb_super_value == cb_target)
03743         return target;
03744         if (cb_super_target == cb_value)
03745         return value;
03746         cb_super_value= cbSuperclass(cb_super_value);
03747         cb_super_target = cbSuperclass(cb_super_target);
03748     }
03749     /* At most one of the following two while clauses will be executed.
03750      * Bring the deeper of cb_target and cb_value to the depth of the
03751      * shallower one.
03752      */
03753     while (cbSuperclass(cb_super_value)) { /* cb_value is deeper */
03754         cb_super_value= cbSuperclass(cb_super_value);
03755         cb_value= cbSuperclass(cb_value);
03756     }
03757     while (cbSuperclass(cb_super_target)) { /* cb_target is deeper */
03758         cb_super_target= cbSuperclass(cb_super_target);
03759         cb_target = cbSuperclass(cb_target);
03760     }
03761 
03762     /* Walk both up, maintaining equal depth, until a join is found.  We
03763      * know that we will find one.  */
03764     while (cb_value != cb_target) {
03765         cb_value =  cbSuperclass(cb_value);
03766         cb_target =  cbSuperclass(cb_target);
03767     }
03768     /* Get the info for this guy.  We know its cb_value, so we should
03769      * fill that in, while we're at it.                      */
03770     value_info = Str2ID_Local(context, &context->classHash,
03771                   cbName(cb_value), &addr, true);
03772     *addr = cb_value;
03773     return MAKE_FULLINFO(ITEM_Object, 0, value_info);
03774     } /* both items are classes */
03775 }
03776 
03777 /*=========================================================================
03778  * FUNCTION:      object_fullinfo_to_classclass
03779  * OVERVIEW:      Given a fullinfo_type corresponding to an Object, return the
03780  *                pointer to the ClassClass structure of that type.
03781  *                Returns 0 for an illegal class.
03782  * INTERFACE:
