03783  *   parameters:  pointer to the context_type
03784  *                fullinfo_type: classinfo
03785  *
03786  *   returns:     pointer to the ClassClass type
03787  *=======================================================================*/
03788 static ClassClass *
03789 object_fullinfo_to_classclass(context_type *context, fullinfo_type classinfo)
03790 {
03791     void **addr;
03792     ClassClass *cb;
03793 
03794     unsigned short info = GET_EXTRA_INFO(classinfo);
03795     char *classname = ID2Str_Local(context, context->classHash, info, &addr);
03796     if ((cb = *addr) != 0) {
03797     return cb;
03798     } else {
03799     *addr = cb = FindClassFromClass(0, classname, false, context->class);
03800     if (cb == 0)
03801         CCerror(context, "Cannot find class %s", classname);
03802     return cb;
03803     }
03804 }
03805 
03806 /*=========================================================================
03807  * The functions below are for debugging the preverifier
03808  *=======================================================================*/
03809 
03810 #ifdef DEBUG_VERIFIER
03811 
03812 static void print_fullinfo_type(context_type *, fullinfo_type, bool);
03813 
03814 /*=========================================================================
03815  * FUNCTION:      print_stack
03816  * OVERVIEW:      Prints stack information.
03817  *
03818  * INTERFACE:
03819  *   parameters:  pointer to the context_type
03820  *                stack_info: pointer to stack_info
03821  *
03822  *   returns:     nothing
03823  *=======================================================================*/
03824 static void
03825 print_stack(context_type *context, stack_info_type *stack_info)
03826 {
03827     stack_item_type *stack = stack_info->stack;
03828     if (stack_info->stack_size == UNKNOWN_STACK_SIZE) {
03829     jio_fprintf(stdout, "x");
03830     } else {
03831     jio_fprintf(stdout, "(");
03832     for ( ; stack != 0; stack = stack->next)
03833         print_fullinfo_type(context, stack->item, verify_verbose > 1);
03834     jio_fprintf(stdout, ")");
03835     }
03836 }
03837 
03838 /*=========================================================================
03839  * FUNCTION:      print_registers
03840  * OVERVIEW:      Prints registers.
03841  *
03842  * INTERFACE:
03843  *   parameters:  pointer to the context_type
03844  *                register_info_type: pointer to register_info
03845  *
03846  *   returns:     nothing
03847  *=======================================================================*/
03848 static void
03849 print_registers(context_type *context, register_info_type *register_info)
03850 {
03851     int register_count = register_info->register_count;
03852     if (register_count == UNKNOWN_REGISTER_COUNT) {
03853     jio_fprintf(stdout, "x");
03854     } else {
03855     fullinfo_type *registers = register_info->registers;
03856     int mask_count = register_info->mask_count;
03857     mask_type *masks = register_info->masks;
03858     int i, j;
03859 
03860     jio_fprintf(stdout, "{");
03861     for (i = 0; i < register_count; i++)
03862         print_fullinfo_type(context, registers[i], verify_verbose > 1);
03863     jio_fprintf(stdout, "}");
03864     for (i = 0; i < mask_count; i++) {
03865         char *separator = "";
03866         int *modifies = masks[i].modifies;
03867         jio_fprintf(stdout, "<%d: ", masks[i].entry);
03868         for (j = 0; j < context->mb->nlocals; j++)
03869         if (IS_BIT_SET(modifies, j)) {
03870             jio_fprintf(stdout, "%s%d", separator, j);
03871             separator = ",";
03872         }
03873         jio_fprintf(stdout, ">");
03874     }
03875     }
03876 }
03877 
03878 /*=========================================================================
03879  * FUNCTION:      print_flags
03880  * OVERVIEW:      Prints flags.
03881  *
03882  * INTERFACE:
03883  *   parameters:  pointer to the context_type
03884  *                flag_type: and_flags
03885  *                flag_type: or_flags
03886  *
03887  *   returns:     nothing
03888  *=======================================================================*/
03889 static void
03890 print_flags(context_type *context, flag_type and_flags, flag_type or_flags)
03891 {
03892     if (and_flags != ((flag_type)-1) || or_flags != 0) {
03893     jio_fprintf(stdout, "<%x %x>", and_flags, or_flags);
03894     }
03895 }
03896 
03897 /*=========================================================================
03898  * FUNCTION:      print_fullinfo_type
03899  * OVERVIEW:      Prints fullinfo_type information.
03900  *
03901  * INTERFACE:
03902  *   parameters:  pointer to the context_type
03903  *                fullinfo_type: type
03904  *                bool_t verbose
03905  *
03906  *   returns:     nothing
03907  *=======================================================================*/
03908 static void
03909 print_fullinfo_type(context_type *context, fullinfo_type type, bool verbose)
03910 {
03911     int i;
03912     int indirection = GET_INDIRECTION(type);
03913     for (i = indirection; i-- > 0; )
03914     jio_fprintf(stdout, "[");
03915     switch (GET_ITEM_TYPE(type)) {
03916         case ITEM_Integer:
03917         jio_fprintf(stdout, "I"); break;
03918     case ITEM_Float:
03919         jio_fprintf(stdout, "F"); break;
03920     case ITEM_Double:
03921         jio_fprintf(stdout, "D"); break;
03922     case ITEM_Double_2:
03923         jio_fprintf(stdout, "d"); break;
03924     case ITEM_Long:
03925         jio_fprintf(stdout, "L"); break;
03926     case ITEM_Long_2:
03927         jio_fprintf(stdout, "l"); break;
03928     case ITEM_ReturnAddress:
03929         jio_fprintf(stdout, "a"); break;
03930     case ITEM_Object:
03931         if (!verbose) {
03932         jio_fprintf(stdout, "A");
03933         } else {
03934         unsigned short extra = GET_EXTRA_INFO(type);
03935         if (extra == 0) {
03936             jio_fprintf(stdout, "/Null/");
03937         } else {
03938             char *name = ID2Str_Local(context, context->classHash,
03939                           extra, 0);
03940             char *name2 = strrchr(name, '/');
03941             jio_fprintf(stdout, "/%s/", name2 ? name2 + 1 : name);
03942         }
03943         }
03944         break;
03945     case ITEM_Char:
03946         jio_fprintf(stdout, "C"); break;
03947     case ITEM_Short:
03948         jio_fprintf(stdout, "S"); break;
03949     case ITEM_Byte:
03950         jio_fprintf(stdout, "B"); break;
03951     case ITEM_Boolean:
03952         jio_fprintf(stdout, "Z"); break;
03953         case ITEM_NewObject:
03954         if (!verbose) {
03955         jio_fprintf(stdout, "@");
03956         } else {
03957         int inum = GET_EXTRA_INFO(type);
03958         fullinfo_type real_type =
03959             context->instruction_data[inum].operand2.fi;
03960         jio_fprintf(stdout, ">");
03961         print_fullinfo_type(context, real_type, true);
03962         jio_fprintf(stdout, "<");
03963         }
03964         break;
03965         case ITEM_InitObject:
03966         jio_fprintf(stdout, verbose ? ">/this/<" : "@");
03967         break;
03968 
03969     default:
03970         jio_fprintf(stdout, "?"); break;
03971     }
03972     for (i = indirection; i-- > 0; )
03973     jio_fprintf(stdout, "]");
03974 }
03975 
03976 /*=========================================================================
03977  * FUNCTION:      print_formatted_fieldname
03978  * OVERVIEW:      Prints formatted fieldname associated with the given
03979  *                index within the constant pool.
03980  *
03981  * INTERFACE:
03982  *   parameters:  pointer to the context_type
03983  *                int: index
03984  *
03985  *   returns:     nothing
03986  *=======================================================================*/
03987 static void
03988 print_formatted_fieldname(context_type *context, int index)
03989 {
03990     union cp_item_type *constant_pool = cbConstantPool(context->class);
03991     unsigned char *type_table =
03992         constant_pool[CONSTANT_POOL_TYPE_TABLE_INDEX].type;
03993     unsigned type = CONSTANT_POOL_TYPE_TABLE_GET_TYPE(type_table, index);
03994 
03995     unsigned key = constant_pool[index].i;
03996     unsigned classkey = key >> 16;
03997     unsigned nametypekey = key & 0xFFFF;
03998     unsigned nametypeindex = constant_pool[nametypekey].i;
03999     unsigned fieldnameindex = nametypeindex >> 16;
04000     unsigned fieldtypeindex = nametypeindex & 0xFFFF;
04001     jio_fprintf(stdout, "  <%s.%s%s%s>",
04002         GetClassConstantClassName(constant_pool, classkey),
04003         constant_pool[fieldnameindex].cp,
04004         type == CONSTANT_Fieldref ? " " : "",
04005         constant_pool[fieldtypeindex].cp);
04006 }
04007 
04008 #endif /*DEBUG_VERIFIER*/
04009
Generated on Mon Mar 14 2016 15:25:27 for @PACKAGE_NAME@ by  1.7.1
